> **Category B status (updated 2026-06-20).** Features 6–8 were re-run cleanly this
> pass — 50 findings survived independent adversarial verification (workflow), now
> listed below. Features 9–12 were again server-throttled out of the parallel verify
> stage, so they were verified by direct source review (✓verified / CLEARED tags).
> Two prior reviewer claims were found already-fixed and cleared (F9 VqcTrainer live
> region, F10 PES aria-valuetext). CORRECTION: the part-1 F6 pass wrongly refuted the
> useWebGL per-render allocation — it is REAL (see Feature 12).

> **2026-06-25 deep audit (Features 6-12).** Exhaustive multi-agent pass: 7 features x 6 lenses + 4 cross-feature sweeps (171 agents), every finding adversarially refuted (file:line real & present now? a refinement not a new feature? already shipped in PRs #34-46?). 125 candidates -> **103 survived, 22 dropped**. After main-thread dedup (the same root cause was surfaced by many agents — aria-live x8, ProbBars x9, card-shell x4, slider x4), the distinct items are recorded below per feature plus a new cross-cutting batch. Two are genuine **teaching-correctness** bugs (VQE landscape drawn upside-down; JW phantom Z-string on the default view) and one is a headline-output formatting bug (Dirac "+ -0.71"); the rest are reuse/a11y/perf/resilience refinements. The widgets remain fundamentally sound — the quantum math is correct and the prior campaign's a11y/perf/consistency work holds; these are the residual edges it did not reach.

### 6. Foundations explorables

- [x] **[Correctness]** ✓verified — **BlochDial silently drops the Y axis**: it computes `{x,y,z}` but plots only `px=c+r·x, py=c−r·z`, so `|i⟩`/`|−i⟩` collapse to the origin (look maximally-mixed) on the no-WebGL / reduced-motion fallback. Encode `y` (equatorial projection or tip marker). Impact: High. Effort: Medium. (`web/src/components/quantum/bloch-dial.tsx:9,13-14,23`) — added 2026-06-17
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE — **Slider-driven P(0)/P(1) + Dirac results now announced to screen readers** via the cross-cutting live-region sweep. Impact: High. Effort: Low. (`bloch-builder-widget.tsx:55-79`, `circuit-lab.tsx:67-90`, `wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:124-148`) — added 2026-06-17

> **Clean verification pass — 2026-06-20.** The adversarial verify stage that was server-throttled on the 2026-06-17 run completed cleanly here (19 findings survived independent skeptic review). _Current state:_ The core quantum math is sound — Bloch vectors and probability bars are correct across all widgets — and the two genuine correctness items found this pass are cosmetic edge cases (a gate-chip label/convention mismatch that only surfaces when cross-referencing the copied amplitudes, and a ~1-in-2^53 zero-probability sampler draw). The largest opportunities are an always-on 3D render loop and substantial DRY cleanup across the circuit-family widgets (gate chips, state readout, probability bars), plus several screen-reader and touch-target accessibility refinements.

- [x] **[Performance]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-1) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **3D Bloch &lt;Canvas&gt; runs a 60fps render loop forever (frameloop defaults to 'always')**. BlochSphere3D mounts a react-three-fiber &lt;Canvas&gt; with no frameloop prop, so it defaults to 'always' and re-renders the WebGL scene 60 times a second for the entire life of the component — even after the StateVector lerp has converged on its target and the user is not dragging OrbitControls. Every Foundations widget that shows the 3D sphere (BlochBuilder, WavefunctionScrubber, ScrollySection) keeps a GPU/CPU loop spinning indefinitely while the visual is completely static, and a lesson page can have several such canvases at once, each with its own independent always-on loop. _Fix:_ In bloch-sphere-3d.tsx, set `frameloop="demand"` on the &lt;Canvas&gt;, and in StateVector pull `invalidate` from `useThree()` and call it from useFrame only while the vector is still moving (e.g. while `current.current.distanceToSquared(target) &gt; epsilon`); also drive renders on interaction by passing `onChange={() =&gt; invalidate()}` (or relying on drei OrbitControls' demand integration). This stops the loop within a few frames of the lerp settling and resumes it on drag or a new target. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:148 - `&lt;Canvas camera={{ position: [1.6, 1.2, 2.2], fov: 45 }} dpr={[1, 2]}&gt;` sets no `frameloop`, so R3F v9 (@react-three/fiber ^9.6.1) uses the default `'always'` continuous render loop · bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:49-58 - `useFrame(() =&gt; { current.current.lerp(target, 0.18); ... })` runs every frame unconditionally with no convergence check; once `current ≈ target` it keeps lerping by a negligible delta forever, so the loop never has a reason to stop on its own · grep across web/src/components/quantum confirms zero `frameloop`/`invalidate` usages anywhere) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-3) — ✓verified — NEW — **Scrubber auto-play floods the polite live region every 750ms**. In WavefunctionScrubber the left column (probability bars + Dirac state string) is wrapped in role="status" aria-live="polite", and the auto-advance effect updates the step every STEP_MS=750ms while playing. Each step re-renders that entire region, so pressing Play queues a fresh verbose announcement (all probability rows plus the Dirac string) every 0.75 seconds for the whole circuit. A screen-reader user gets a relentless, un-interruptible backlog of readouts rather than a single useful update. _Fix:_ In wavefunction-scrubber.tsx, suppress live-region announcements during continuous playback: drop aria-live to "off" (or remove role="status") while isPlaying is true and restore it for manual scrubbing/keyboard step changes, OR move the live announcement out of the visual block into a single dedicated sr-only status node that only updates on settle (e.g. when playback pauses or reaches lastStep). Keep the existing polite readout for slider/keyboard-driven single-step changes. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:123 - the readout column is `&lt;div className="min-w-0 flex-1" role="status" aria-live="polite"&gt;` containing the probability bars and Dirac string · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:23 - `const STEP_MS = 750;` · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:58-62 - the play effect calls `setTimeout(() =&gt; setStep((s) =&gt; Math.min(s + 1, lastStep)), STEP_MS)`, re-rendering the live region on every frame while playing) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **Correlation demo announces the full tally but not the per-measurement outcome**. In CorrelationDemo each panel's 4-row tally table carries role="status" (implicit aria-live="polite"), so every click of Measure makes both panels read out all four count/percentage rows twice. Meanwhile the single most informative result of a measurement — the "Last: |00&gt;" outcome line — sits in a plain div with no live region, so the actual sampled outcome is never announced. Two simultaneous polite regions also compete on each click. _Fix:_ In correlation-demo.tsx, remove role="status" from each panel's tally table and instead announce a single concise summary per Measure via one sr-only role="status" region at the component level (e.g. "Entangled measured 00, product measured 11; 12 measurements total"). Leave the tally bars as static visuals. This announces the useful event once instead of reading 8 tally rows. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (correlation-demo.tsx:69 - `&lt;div role="status" className="space-y-1.5"&gt;` wraps the four-row tally for each of the two panels · correlation-demo.tsx:58-66 - the "Last: |...&gt;" outcome line is in a bare `&lt;div className="mb-3 font-mono ..."&gt;` with no role/aria-live · correlation-demo.tsx:131-139 - handleMeasure updates entangledLast, productLast and both tallies on every click, firing both panels' status regions at once) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **Scroll-driven explorable never announces beat changes to screen readers**. The ScrollySection Explorable updates the active beat caption and the "Beat N / M" counter as the user scrolls, but neither the caption container nor the counter is a live region, and there is no aria-valuetext/role on the scroll progress. A sighted user sees the caption cross-fade and the progress rail grow; a screen-reader user who scrolls gets no notification that the state/beat changed. Non-active captions are correctly aria-hidden (line 117), so the only readable text is whichever beat happens to be active, with no announcement when it flips. _Fix:_ In scrolly-section.tsx Explorable, wrap the "Beat N / M" + active caption in a single role="status" aria-live="polite" region (or add an sr-only status node updated on activeBeatIndex change) so beat transitions are announced. This refines the existing scroll explorable without adding scope; the StaticBeats fallback (reduced-motion/no-WebGL) already exposes all captions and is unaffected. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (scrolly-section.tsx:110-112 - the `Beat {active + 1} / {beats.length}` counter is a plain span with no aria-live · scrolly-section.tsx:113-127 - the captions container has no role/aria-live; only `aria-hidden` toggles per caption · scrolly-section.tsx grep for aria-live/role/aria-label returned no matches in the Explorable) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-2a) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **Gate-chip label + markup triplicated across the circuit-family widgets**. The exact gate-to-label switch (CNOT / bound θ / fixed angle / plain) and its chip `&lt;span&gt;` markup are copied verbatim into three of this feature's files, and bloch-builder-widget hand-inlines the same chip class with no label logic. No shared GateChip / GateChips component exists, so the label rules and the pill styling must be kept in sync by hand across every widget (the same class string also recurs in ~20 sibling explorers). _Fix:_ Extract a single `GateChip` (one pill, optional `active` prop) and a `gateLabel(g)` helper — or a `&lt;GateChips program=... activeIndex?=... /&gt;` component — into a shared module (e.g. a new gate-chips.tsx beside qsim-dsl.ts) and import it from circuit-lab.tsx, correlation-demo.tsx, wavefunction-scrubber.tsx, and bloch-builder-widget.tsx. The scrubber's active-state highlight becomes the optional prop. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (circuit-lab.tsx:33-50 - builds `gateChips` with the CNOT/bound/angle/plain label switch and the `rounded-chip bg-gray-100 ...` span · correlation-demo.tsx:17-36 - `gateChips(program)` repeats the identical label switch and identical span markup · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:89-111 - repeats the same label switch verbatim (only adding an `active` highlight branch) · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:45 - inlines the same `rounded-chip bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800 px-2 py-0.5 text-[11px] font-mono ...` chip class by hand) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-2a) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **Dirac state + dual copy-button readout block is byte-identical in three widgets**. The |ψ⟩ = diracString(...) line paired with the two CopyButtons (notation + the `py`-badged Python copy) is copy-pasted identically into circuit-lab, wavefunction-scrubber, and bloch-builder-widget. The state-readout.ts module already single-sources the string formatting, but the surrounding JSX (the `mt-4 flex items-start gap-2` wrapper, both CopyButton labels, the py badge markup) is not componentized, so any change to the readout's layout or labels must be made in three places. _Fix:_ Extract a `StateReadout` component (props: `state: Complex[]`, `n: number`) into state-readout.ts's sibling (or a new state-readout.tsx) that renders the Dirac line plus both CopyButtons and the py badge, and use it in all three widgets. The three call sites collapse to `&lt;StateReadout state={...} n={...} /&gt;`. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (circuit-lab.tsx:86-98 - `|ψ⟩ = ` line + CopyButton(diracString) + CopyButton(toPythonState) + `py` badge · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:142-156 - same block, only the state source differs (`current` vs `sim.state!`) · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:75-87 - same block again with `state` and `n=1`) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-07-04 (WS-B4, PR #93 — shared `Bar` extended with ReactNode valueText, `valueWidth`, a typed `marker` slot (overflow derived from marker presence), and `ariaLabel` as a named role="img" row; shots-sampler + correlation-demo rows rebuilt on it; shots also migrated to WidgetCard/ErrorCard — the widget WS-6c missed. Unifications: shots track h-4→h-3, label w-10→w-12, correlation gains motion-reduce + 200ms) — PARTIAL 2026-06-20 (WS-2a: shared `ProbBars` extracted for circuit-lab/scrubber/bloch-builder; shots/correlation empirical+marker variants still custom) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **Probability-bar row reimplemented in every readout widget instead of a shared ProbBars component**. The basis-label + track + accent fill + right-hand percentage row is reimplemented in circuit-lab, wavefunction-scrubber, and bloch-builder-widget with identical structure and Tailwind classes, and shots-sampler / correlation-demo render a near-identical row (empirical fill + count/percent variant). There is no shared bar-row primitive, so the bar geometry (`h-3`, `rounded-full bg-accent`, width-from-percent, `w-12` label columns) is duplicated and drifts (shots-sampler uses `h-4`/`overflow-visible`, others `h-3`/`overflow-hidden`). _Fix:_ Extract a `ProbBar` (one row: label, fill width, right readout) — and optionally a `ProbBars` list wrapper — into a shared module, parameterized for the simple fill (circuit/scrubber/bloch) and the empirical+marker overlay (shots/correlation). Import it from all five widgets so the bar geometry has one source of truth. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (circuit-lab.tsx:69-84 - probability bar row: basisLabel span + track span + `bg-accent` fill + `(p*100).toFixed(1)%` · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:125-139 - identical probability bar row · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:56-71 - identical probability bar row · shots-sampler.tsx:99-131 - same row shape with empirical fill + exact-marker variant (`h-4`, `overflow-visible`) · correlation-demo.tsx:70-96 - same tally-row shape with count/percent readout) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Correctness]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-2b) — ✓verified — NEW — **BlochBuilder's 'RY θ RZ φ' gate-chip mislabels a state computed with a different phase convention**. The widget shows a gate-sequence chip reading 'RY θ RZ φ' (implying the state is RZ(φ)·RY(θ)|0&gt;), but the Dirac string and copy-as-Python output are computed by stateFromAngles, which uses the convention cos(θ/2)|0&gt; + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1&gt; (the entire relative phase placed on |1&gt;). RZ(φ)·RY(θ)|0&gt; actually equals e^{-iφ/2}cos(θ/2)|0&gt; + e^{+iφ/2}sin(θ/2)|1&gt;, which differs by a non-trivial per-amplitude phase split. A learner who runs the labeled gate sequence in the WavefunctionScrubber (qsim 'RY 0 θ / RZ 0 φ') or pastes the labeled gates into Python gets a different amplitude string than the widget's own readout — e.g. for θ=π/2, φ=π/2 the widget prints 0.71|0&gt; + 0.71i|1&gt; while the labeled gates give (0.5-0.5i)|0&gt; + (0.5+0.5i)|1&gt;. _Fix:_ In bloch-builder.ts, make stateFromAngles actually apply RZ(φ)·RY(θ)|0&gt; (reuse scrolly.ts stateForAngles / math.ts ry+rz so all foundations explorables share one convention), OR change the chip label in bloch-builder-widget.tsx:33 to reflect the canonical-phase convention it currently uses (e.g. drop the gate-sequence framing and label it |ψ⟩ = cos(θ/2)|0&gt; + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1&gt;). The Bloch vector is identical either way, so only the displayed/copied amplitudes change; aligning with scrolly.ts's real-gate convention also makes the qbloch and qscrub widgets agree. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (bloch-builder-widget.tsx:33 - const gateSeq = `RY ${theta.toFixed(2)}  RZ ${phi.toFixed(2)}`; advertises the state as RZ(φ)RY(θ)|0&gt; · bloch-builder.ts:4-11 - stateFromAngles returns [c,0] and [s*cos(phi), s*sin(phi)], i.e. cos(θ/2)|0&gt; + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1&gt;, phase entirely on |1&gt; (no e^{-iφ/2} on |0&gt;) · math.ts:54-58 - rz(theta) = diag(c-si, c+si) = diag(e^{-iφ/2}, e^{+iφ/2}); applied as RZ(φ)RY(θ)|0&gt; this splits the phase across both amplitudes, not matching stateFromAngles · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:78-83 - the Dirac string and Copy-as-Python button both serialize this non-RZ·RY state, so the copied artifact disagrees with the displayed gate sequence and with the scrubber running the same labeled gates) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Correctness]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-2b) — ✓verified — NEW — **Inverse-transform samplers can emit a zero-probability basis state when rng() returns exactly 0**. Both Born-rule samplers select an outcome with the predicate `r &lt;= acc` where r = rng()*total. Math.random() can legitimately return exactly 0, giving r=0, which satisfies `0 &lt;= cdf[0]` and returns index 0 even when probs[0] === 0. For a circuit whose first basis state has zero amplitude (e.g. an entangled state with probabilities [0, 0.5, 0.5, 0], or a product state with no |00&gt; component), this yields a physically impossible measurement outcome — the CorrelationDemo can tally a |00&gt; count for a Bell-like state that should never produce it, and the ShotsSampler can place a shot on a zero-probability bar. _Fix:_ In shots.ts and correlation.ts, skip zero-probability buckets — either advance past any leading prob===0 entries before returning, or change the cumulative comparison so an outcome with zero mass is never selected (e.g. accumulate and return the first index where acc &gt; r with a strict comparison, falling back to the last non-zero bucket). A single shared sampler used by both call sites would fix it in one place. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (correlation.ts:25-30 - sampleOutcome: `const r = rng()*total; ... if (r &lt;= acc) return i;` returns i=0 whenever r=0 regardless of whether probs[0] is 0 · shots.ts:11-17 - sampleCounts binary search with `if (r &lt;= cdf[mid]) hi = mid` collapses to lo=0 when r=0, even if cdf[0]===0 (probs[0]===0) · correlation-demo.tsx:132-137 - handleMeasure feeds these indices straight into the visible tally, so a zero-probability |00&gt;/|11&gt; can be displayed for the entangled panel) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Performance]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-1) — ✓verified — NEW — **Three great-circle point arrays are rebuilt on every state change in Scene**. Scene calls circlePoints('equator'/'xz'/'yz') directly in JSX with no memoization, each building a fresh 65-element array of 3-tuples. Scene re-renders whenever the `state` prop changes — which happens on every slider drag tick in BlochBuilder and every scrub step / scroll rAF in WavefunctionScrubber and ScrollySection — so these three static ring geometries are reallocated and the drei &lt;Line&gt; meshes regenerate their geometry on input that never actually moves them. _Fix:_ In bloch-sphere-3d.tsx, hoist the three circlePoints(...) results and the static axis-line point literals to module-level constants (they never depend on state), or wrap them in useMemo with an empty dep list inside Scene. The rings and axes are fixed geometry; only StateVector should react to `state`. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:102-104 - `&lt;Line points={circlePoints("equator")} ... /&gt;`, `circlePoints("xz")`, `circlePoints("yz")` are invoked inline on each Scene render; circlePoints (lines 29-40) loops 65 times allocating `[number,number,number]` tuples each call · bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:92-93 - Scene receives `state` and only `target` is memoized (`useMemo(() =&gt; blochToThree(state), [state])`); the circle points and axis-line literals are not, so any new `state` array re-runs the whole Scene body · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:92 / wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:161 / scrolly-section.tsx:107 - all pass a freshly-computed `state` into &lt;BlochSphere3D&gt; on every drag/scrub/scroll update, driving the unmemoized re-renders) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Performance]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-1) — ✓verified — NEW — **usePrefersReducedMotion getSnapshot allocates a MediaQueryList on every call**. The getSnapshot passed to useSyncExternalStore re-invokes window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') on every read, allocating a fresh MediaQueryList object each time just to read `.matches`. React calls getSnapshot on every render and after every store notification across all consumers (BlochBuilder, WavefunctionScrubber, ScrollySection). Because it returns a boolean primitive the value stays stable, so this is wasted allocation rather than a correctness bug, but it is on a hot path during scrubbing and scrolling. _Fix:_ In use-display-caps.ts, create one module-level MediaQueryList (`const reducedMq = window.matchMedia(...)` guarded for SSR) and have both subscribe and getSnapshot reference it, returning `reducedMq.matches`. This removes the per-read allocation while keeping the stable boolean snapshot. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (use-display-caps.ts:15 - getSnapshot is `() =&gt; window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches`, constructing a new MediaQueryList per call instead of reusing the one already created in the subscribe closure (line 11) · wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:44 and scrolly-section.tsx:143 - both call usePrefersReducedMotion(), and ScrollySection re-renders on every scroll rAF (progress state), so getSnapshot runs on each of those renders) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Performance]** DONE 2026-06-20 (WS-1) — ✓verified (corrected 2026-06-20) — **useWebGL allocates a canvas + WebGL context on every render** (the part-1 refutation here was wrong). `useSyncExternalStore` calls getSnapshot (`detectWebGL`) on every render, not only on store change, and `detectWebGL` allocates a canvas + GL context each call; the consumers (this widget, wavefunction-scrubber, scrolly-section) re-render on drag/scrub/scroll, so GL contexts churn during interaction. See Feature 12 for the canonical entry + fix (memoize WebGL detection at module scope). Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`use-display-caps.ts:20-27,29-36`) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **ShotsSampler buttons lack the shared focus-ring and accessible labels**. Every other interactive control in this feature (sliders use .focus-ring, CorrelationDemo and the scrubber play button use `interactive focus-ring`, CopyButton uses them too) gets the project's high-contrast focus-visible ring. The ShotsSampler preset buttons and the Run button use none of those utilities, so they fall back to whatever the browser default outline is and visibly break the feature's focus-visibility consistency. The preset buttons also have no text beyond a bare number and no aria-label, and Run is unlabeled beyond its text. _Fix:_ In shots-sampler.tsx add `interactive focus-ring` to the preset buttons and Run button (matching CorrelationDemo's buttons), and give the preset buttons an aria-label like `aria-label={`${n} shots`}` since aria-pressed already conveys selection. Purely additive class/attribute change, no behavior change. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (shots-sampler.tsx:69-82 - preset buttons class list is `"rounded px-2.5 py-1 text-xs font-mono font-medium transition-colors"` plus selected/unselected colors, with no `focus-ring`/`interactive` · shots-sampler.tsx:83-88 - Run button class is `"ml-2 rounded px-3 py-1 text-xs font-semibold bg-accent text-white hover:bg-accent-dark transition-colors"`, no focus-ring · globals.css:262-268 - `.focus-ring` / `.interactive` utilities are the project-wide focus + press affordances applied by the other controls) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **Range slider thumb is a 16px target, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum**. The shared .slider thumb (used by every theta/phi/scrub slider in the feature) is rendered at 16x16px on a 6px-tall track. WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.5.8 (Target Size, Minimum) calls for at least 24x24 CSS px for pointer targets; a 16px draggable thumb on a thin rail is hard to grab on touch and for users with motor impairments. The sliders are keyboard-operable (so 2.5.8's exceptions partially apply), but the pointer affordance is undersized. _Fix:_ In globals.css enlarge the .slider thumb to at least 24x24px (or 20px thumb + a taller hit area via padding/`::-webkit-slider-runnable-track` height) for the webkit and moz thumb rules; this lifts every Foundations slider above the touch-target floor at once without touching component code. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (globals.css:274-283 - `.slider::-webkit-slider-thumb { width: 16px; height: 16px; ... }` · globals.css:284-292 - `.slider::-moz-range-thumb { width: 16px; height: 16px; ... }` · globals.css:271-273 - `.slider { @apply h-1.5 ... }` makes the track only 6px tall · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:111 / circuit-lab.tsx:118 / wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:193 - all sliders use `className="slider flex-1 focus-ring"`) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **ShotsSampler exact-probability marker conveys its value only via title tooltip**. The thin vertical line marking each outcome's exact probability exposes its numeric value solely through a native `title` attribute, which is mouse-hover-only and not reliably surfaced to keyboard or screen-reader users. The empirical/exact percentages are also shown in the right-hand text readout, so the value is not entirely lost, but the on-bar marker itself is inaccessible and the legend only labels what the line means, not its position. _Fix:_ In shots-sampler.tsx, since the exact percentage is already in the visible text readout, simply drop the title-only dependency for accessibility (or pair the histogram row with an aria-label on the row container summarizing "|00&gt;: empirical X%, exact Y%"). Refines the existing readout; no new UI. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (shots-sampler.tsx:112-117 - the exact-probability marker is `&lt;span className="absolute ... w-0.5 ..." style={{ left: ... }} title={`Exact: ${exactPct}%`} /&gt;` · shots-sampler.tsx:121-130 - the same exactPct is redundantly available in the visible text readout column) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[UX]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5a) — ✓verified — NEW — **Shots sampler has no empty-state prompt before the first Run**. Before any Run, the histogram already renders one row per basis state showing the exact-probability marker line and the exact % on the right, but the empirical bars are zero-width and there is no instruction telling the learner what to do. A first-time reader sees a static chart with thin marker lines and may not realize the whole point is to press Run and watch empirical frequencies converge. The '{total} shots' counter is also hidden until total&gt;0, so the control area gives no hint of its purpose at rest. _Fix:_ In shots-sampler.tsx, when `total === 0` show a one-line muted hint near the histogram or in the right-hand readout column (e.g. 'Press Run to sample N shots and compare to the exact probability'). This clarifies the flow without adding any feature; it reuses the existing exact-probability layout already drawn at rest. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (shots-sampler.tsx:93-130 - the histogram maps over `probs` and renders the exact marker + `{exactPct}%` even when `total === 0` (empirical fill width is 0), with no placeholder caption · shots-sampler.tsx:58-62 - the `{total} shots` badge only renders `{total &gt; 0 &amp;&amp; ...}`, so nothing labels the resting state · shots-sampler.tsx:66-67 - the controls row starts with just 'Shots:' and preset chips, no hint that Run drives the empirical comparison) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-2b) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **Two Born-rule samplers exist for the same single-draw operation**. shots.ts `sampleCounts` and correlation.ts `sampleOutcome` both inverse-transform-sample a basis-state index from a `probs[]` array; `sampleCounts` does it n times with a binary-search CDF, `sampleOutcome` does one linear-scan draw. They are two implementations of the same Born-rule draw with divergent code (and `sampleCounts` builds its own CDF inline rather than reusing a shared single-draw primitive), so the sampling logic is maintained twice. _Fix:_ Define one `sampleIndex(cdf|probs, rng)` primitive (or have `sampleCounts` call `sampleOutcome` in a loop) in a single module (e.g. shots.ts) and have correlation.ts import it, so there is one Born-rule draw implementation. Keep `sampleCounts`'s batched CDF as the perf path that the single-draw helper shares. Coordinate with the zero-probability sampler fix so the consolidated primitive also skips zero-mass buckets. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (shots.ts:5-20 - `sampleCounts(probs,n,rng)` builds a CDF and binary-searches it n times to tally counts · correlation.ts:25-31 - `sampleOutcome(probs,rng)` linearly scans the same `probs` for one draw) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-2b) — ✓verified — NEW — **Two divergent angle-to-single-qubit-state functions with different amplitude conventions**. bloch-builder.ts `stateFromAngles(θ,φ)` returns cos(θ/2)|0⟩ + e^{iφ}sin(θ/2)|1⟩ (zero phase on |0⟩), while scrolly.ts `stateForAngles(θ,φ)` builds RZ(φ)·RY(θ)|0⟩, which carries a symmetric e^{∓iφ/2} global/relative phase split. Both map to the same Bloch vector (so the spheres agree), but they are two separate implementations of 'single-qubit state from Bloch angles' that produce different raw amplitude arrays — and only one feeds the copy-as-Python / Dirac readout, so the two declared sources of truth for this state can disagree on the literal amplitudes a learner copies. _Fix:_ Pick one canonical angle-to-state helper in a shared module (the math.ts kernel or bloch-builder.ts) and have both bloch-builder-widget and scrolly.ts import it, so the amplitude convention is single-sourced. If the RZ·RY phase split is intentional for the scrolly path, document why and still route both through one function with an explicit phase-convention flag. This is the same root cause as the BlochBuilder gate-chip mislabel correctness item — fixing one fixes both. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (bloch-builder.ts:4-11 - `stateFromAngles` returns `[c,0]` and `[s·cosφ, s·sinφ]` directly · scrolly.ts:61-66 - `stateForAngles` builds the state via `applyGate1(ry(theta))` then `applyGate1(rz(phi))` · bloch-builder-widget.tsx:29 - feeds `stateFromAngles(theta,phi)` into diracString/toPythonState copy buttons · scrolly.ts:68-70 - `stateForBeat` feeds `stateForAngles` into BlochDial via scrolly-section.tsx:54) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-2b) — ✓verified — NEW — **bloch-dial reimplements zeroState(1) and a unit-clamp the kernel could provide**. bloch-dial.tsx hand-declares `ZERO_STATE` as a literal `[[1,0],[0,0]]` default and a local `clampUnit` (Math.max(-1,Math.min(1,...))), while math.ts already exports `zeroState(n)` for the |0⟩ state and scrolly-section.tsx separately defines its own `clamp01`. The |0⟩ literal and the clamp helpers are small but re-declared rather than reused, so the dial's default state and the family's clamping live in their own copies. _Fix:_ Default `bloch-dial.tsx`'s `state` to `zeroState(1)` from math.ts instead of the local `ZERO_STATE`, and move a shared `clamp(v,min,max)` (or `clamp01`/`clampUnit`) into math.ts so bloch-dial.tsx and scrolly-section.tsx import one implementation. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (bloch-dial.tsx:3-7 - local `ZERO_STATE` literal and `clampUnit` helper · math.ts:65-69 - `zeroState(n)` already produces the |0⟩ state vector · scrolly-section.tsx:31 - a separate `clamp01` helper duplicating the same clamp idiom (also in scrolly.ts:82,96 inline)) — added 2026-06-20

_New (2026-06-25 deep audit):_
- [x] **[UX]** DONE 2026-06-26 (WS-6g, #64 — fallback dial now renders size={180} and the 3D root is a fixed `h-[180px] w-[180px] shrink-0` box; regression tests in bloch-builder-widget/wavefunction-scrubber test files; stale checkbox caught by the 2026-07-03 /plan verification; 2026-07-04 WS-B2/PR #91 addendum: all three `BlochSphere3D` dynamic() imports now reserve the 180px footprint via a `loading` placeholder, closing the residual cold-cache pop-in — review extended it to scrolly-section too) — **Bloch widgets shift layout ~48px when the 132px dial upgrades to the 180px 3D sphere after hydration.** `useWebGL`/`usePrefersReducedMotion` return `false` on the server + first paint, so the fallback `BlochDial` renders at its default 132px; `useSyncExternalStore` then re-renders post-hydration and swaps in the 180×180 `BlochSphere3D` with no reserved space — a visible CLS/flicker on essentially every page with a single-qubit builder or scrubber. Fix: pass `size={180}` to the fallback dial (it already scales geometry from `size`) or wrap the `show3D` ternary in a fixed `h-[180px] w-[180px] shrink-0` box. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`bloch-builder-widget.tsx:54-58`, `wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:105-110`, `bloch-dial.tsx:17-25`, `bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:171-180`) — added 2026-06-25
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-25 (WS-6b, #62 merged) — **Shots sampler announces nothing on Run** — the one circuit-family widget WS-3/WS-5a missed. Pressing Run rewrites the histogram but the component has no `role="status"`/`aria-live` anywhere, and per-bar descriptions sit on `aria-label`'d bare `<div>`s (no role) outside any live region. Add an sr-only `role="status" aria-live="polite"` summary on Run, mirroring `correlation-demo.tsx:134-138`. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`shots-sampler.tsx:29-33,107-114`) — added 2026-06-25 _(see cross-cutting aria-live item)_
- [x] **[Performance]** CLOSED 2026-07-04 as mitigated/by-design (WS-B2, PR #91 verification): the labels are React.memo'd as a cluster (KetLabels, #64), the Canvas is `frameloop="demand"` (#34, zero idle frames), and drei 10.7.7's eps-gate skips the DOM `style.transform` write when the projected position is unchanged — so lerp frames cost 6 cheap projections with no DOM writes, and during orbit drag the writes are NECESSARY (camera rotation genuinely moves the labels). The ledger's suggested fixes do not fit: `occlude` ADDS per-frame raycasts, `sprite` forces the heavier CSS3D path, and drei `<Text>`/troika fetches its font from a CDN (violates the zero-third-party e2e). Future option if profiling ever shows label cost: ONE custom overlay useFrame that early-returns on unchanged camera.matrixWorld and writes 6 span refs. — **The six drei `<Html>` ket labels reproject to the DOM every frame during 3D interaction.** Each `<Html>` overlay recomputes its screen position on every render of the always-on/​on-demand loop while the user drags, even though the labels are anchored to fixed axes. Gate them behind `occlude`/`sprite` or memoize their positions so they aren't reprojected per frame. Impact: Low. Effort: Medium. (`bloch-sphere-3d.tsx` — the `<Html>` ket labels) — added 2026-06-25
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-07-04 (WS-B2, PR #91 — `blochVectorSR` in format.ts single-sources the dial's exact label; sr-only `BlochVectorSR` rendered beside the aria-hidden canvas in ALL THREE consumers incl. scrolly-section, which this item had missed; kept outside every aria-live region; three mocked-3D test suites pin it) — **Swapping in the 3D sphere drops the dial's Bloch-vector text equivalent.** `BlochDial` exposes the vector to AT, but when `show3D` is true the `BlochSphere3D` canvas replaces it with no equivalent text, so SR users on WebGL devices lose the ⟨x,y,z⟩ readout the dial provided. Render the sr-only vector text in both branches. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`bloch-builder-widget.tsx:54-58`, `bloch-sphere-3d.tsx`) — added 2026-06-25
- [x] **[Resilience & tests]** DONE 2026-07-04 (WS-B3, PR #92 — sampleIndex rng()===0 leading-zero pin, sole-bucket sampleCounts case, tail-accumulation check, sampleOutcome re-export pin; the sampler is now linear-scan `sampleIndex`, not the old binary-search) — **The `shots.ts` zero-probability/`r===0` invariant (fixed in WS-2b) has no regression test**, so the leading-zero-bucket guard can silently regress. Add a unit test that drives `rng()===0` against a `probs=[0, .5, .5, 0]` distribution. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`shots.ts:5-20`, `tests`) — added 2026-06-25
- [x] **[Consistency]** DONE 2026-06-26 (WS-6h, #67 merged) — `scrolly-section` eyebrow `text-accent-dark` → `text-accent` to match sibling explorables. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`scrolly-section.tsx`) — added 2026-06-25



> **2026-07-19 Category B re-audit** — 17 adversarially-verified findings (independent skeptic per claim, default-refute; correctness claims re-derived from first principles, one by porting the shipped code to a measurement harness).

- [x] **[Accessibility]** Light-theme non-text accent graphics sit below the 3:1 WCAG 1.4.11 floor (only the text tier was re-routed) — PR #172's token work proved the light `--accent` computes 2.79:1 on `--surface-base` and re-routed resting accent TEXT to `--accent-dark` (4.93:1), with token-contrast.test.ts pinning it. But the F6 explorables' meaningful GRAPHICS still ride the raw light accent, under the 3:1 non-text minimum: the exact-probability marker line inside Bar (the shots sampler's whole teaching point — spot the exact value against the empirical bar), the matching legend swatch, the BlochDial state-vector line/tip (currentColor from `text-accent`), and the shared `.slider` thumb — the primary grab affordance of every θ/φ/scrub slider — whose fill is `var(--color-accent)` and whose 1px `--bd-2` halo composites to ~1.6:1. Low-vision users in the light theme can lose the marker, the dial vector, and the slider thumb. _Fix:_ Pair the light theme down one step exactly like the text tier: `bg-accent-dark dark:bg-accent-light` for the Bar marker and shots legend swatch, `text-accent-dark dark:text-accent` on the BlochDial svg, and point the slider-thumb fill at `var(--focus)` (already per-theme: accent-dark on light at 4.93:1, bright accent on dark at 12.8:1). Extend token-contrast.test.ts with a graphics-tier assertion (&gt;= 3:1) so the next token retune cannot regress it. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:116`, `web/src/components/quantum/shots-sampler.tsx:141`, `web/src/components/quantum/bloch-dial.tsx:75-77,115-116`, `web/src/app/globals.css:522-544`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** 3D Bloch sphere accent color goes stale on theme toggle (post-#169 drift) — BlochSphere3D resolves the accent color exactly once, at mount: `const [accent] = useState(resolveAccent)` probes a `text-accent` span and never re-resolves. Before the Instrument restyle this was correct — the old `--color-accent: oklch(0.72 0.15 192)` was a compile-time @theme value identical in both themes. Since #169 the accent is a runtime per-theme var (light `oklch(0.66 0.1 118)`, dark `oklch(0.85 0.11 118)`). A reader who toggles dark→light while a sphere is mounted (qbloch builder, qscrub scrubber, or the qscrolly sticky sphere — none of which remount on theme change) keeps the bright dark-theme olive for the state vector and target ghost, which is nearly invisible (~1.6:1) on the light glass card; light→dark keeps the dim olive. Every surrounding 2D element (BlochDial, bars, chips) updates instantly, so the sphere is visibly out of system. The catch-fallback `"#2cc9d6"` is also the retired pre-#169 cyan brand color. _Fix:_ Re-resolve the accent when the resolved theme changes: read `resolvedTheme` from next-themes' useTheme() inside this client-only component and re-run resolveAccent in an effect keyed on it (or subscribe a MutationObserver to the documentElement class attribute, matching how use-display-caps subscribes to media changes). The color flows in as a prop, so R3F's demand-mode rendering picks up the change via the normal re-render. While there, update the catch fallback from the retired cyan "#2cc9d6" to an olive literal matching the current tokens. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:184-199`, `web/src/components/quantum/bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:202`, `web/src/app/globals.css:154`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** BlochDial's in-SVG ket axis labels are gray-400 on the light glass (~2.5:1) and fork from the 3D branch's token-inked labels — The 2D Bloch dial's visible axis labels |0&gt;, |1&gt;, |+&gt;, |-&gt; are drawn at fill-gray-400 with no dark: variant and no token routing (bloch-dial.tsx:118-121, unchanged since PR #1; WS-3/WS-B1 never touched these lines). On the light theme the glass card sits on --surface-1 = oklch(0.99 0.003 88) (globals.css:160), so #9ca3af text computes to roughly 2.5:1 — far below the 4.5:1 WCAG 1.4.3 floor for 9-12px text. Meanwhile the 3D sphere's equivalent ket labels were already re-routed to the AA-safe caption token (bloch-sphere-3d.tsx:112 uses text-caption, which globals.css:547 maps to --mut at 6.5:1), so the same teaching labels pass in one branch and fail in the sibling branch every reduced-motion/no-WebGL user actually gets. This is the text tier the existing '[UI] light-theme non-text accent graphics below 3:1' candidate explicitly excludes. _Fix:_ Replace fill-gray-400 on the four ket &lt;text&gt; labels with the caption ink token (fill-(--mut), matching the .text-caption tier the 3D labels already use), keeping the hub dot's existing gray pair or tokenizing it the same way. One-line class change per label; the dial's role="img" aria-label is untouched, so no test churn beyond a class assertion if one exists. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. Inside one card the slider readout renders cool #6b7280-family gray while the header count two lines away renders warm --mut, and any future token retune (like #172's) silently skips these six sites. This is distinct from the recorded candidates, which target the ErrorCard clones, the skeleton card, and the eyebrow fork — not the shared primitives' own ink. _Fix:_ Mechanical re-route of the six sites to the existing tier: text-(--ink) for the Dirac line, text-(--mut)/text-caption for slider label+value and ErrorCard, bg-(--field)/border-(--bd) equivalents inside secondaryActionClass/fieldClass, and a token hover (e.g. hover:bg-(--field)) on Reset. No new tokens, no behavior change; the token-contrast test suite added in #172 already covers the target tier. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. Minor, but it is duplicated logic with three chances to typo a bound in a file family that prizes single-sourcing. _Fix:_ Add clamp to scrolly.ts's existing math.ts import and use clamp(progress01, 0, 1) in both functions; have scrolly-section.tsx import the same (or reuse a clamp01 exported from scrolly.ts) and delete the local arrow. - [x] **[Accessibility]** Scrubber play/pause button combines aria-pressed with a swapping accessible name — The WavefunctionScrubber's play control swaps its aria-label between "Play animation" and "Pause animation" AND toggles aria-pressed. ARIA APG guidance for toggle buttons is to pick one: with both, a screen reader announces "Pause animation, toggle button, pressed" while playing — 'Pause…pressed' reads as 'paused is engaged', the opposite of the actual state. The label swap alone (the standard media-player pattern for an icon button whose action inverts) is unambiguous. This has survived since the widget's first commit and was not covered by the recorded live-region/WS-5a closures. _Fix:_ Drop `aria-pressed` and keep the action-named label swap (Play animation / Pause animation), matching the convention for media transport controls; the icon change plus name change fully conveys state without the contradictory pressed announcement. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/wavefunction-scrubber.tsx:140-141`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)

PR #172's sweep synced the color half of these two copies (`text-accent` → `text-accent-dark dark:text-accent`) but not the weight, proving the duplication actively drifts: the qscrolly card header renders visibly bolder than the qbloch/qsim/qshots headers directly above and below it. _Fix:_ Replace both literal strings with `<EyebrowLabel>` from widget-ui (the Explorable counter needs only its existing block/mb spacing kept on a wrapper), erasing the weight drift and making future eyebrow retunes single-sourced. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[UX]** ShotsSampler shows the same shot count in three formats — The empty-state hint formats the pending count with locale grouping — "Press Run to sample 10,000 shots" (bare `toLocaleString()`, also diverging from the codebase's explicit `toLocaleString("en-US")` idiom) — while after Run the header chip renders raw `{total}` ("10000 shots") and the screen-reader LiveStatus announces "Sampled 10000 shots". At the two largest presets the same number is shown grouped before Run and ungrouped after, an avoidable inconsistency in a widget whose entire subject is the shot count. The #79 numeric-consistency sweep single-sourced percent/radian readouts but missed the count tier here. _Fix:_ Format the count once — apply `toLocaleString("en-US")` to the header chip and the LiveStatus string, and pin the hint to the same explicit locale — so "10,000 shots" is identical before Run, in the chip, and in the SR announcement. Impact: Low. Effort: Low.
