- [x] **[UX]** VQC loss curve inverts its meaning after ~7 Train clicks — a converged run renders pinned at the TOP of the plot — The loss curve auto-fits its y-domain to whatever is currently inside a 240-sample sliding window. Once the initial high loss scrolls out of that window, the domain max collapses to the near-zero converged residual, so a fully-trained model's flat loss is rescaled to the top edge of the box — visually identical to "loss is at maximum, nothing is being learned". The widget's own caption promises the opposite ("watch the boundary sharpen as the curve descends"). There are no axis ticks, no min/max labels, and no zero line, so nothing anchors the reading. The SVG's aria-label also saturates while the visible step counter keeps climbing, so the announced and displayed step counts diverge. _Fix:_ Anchor the y-domain to the run's all-time maximum instead of the visible window: carry a `lossMax` alongside `history` (seeded in the `useState` initializer at line 204, extended in `onTrain`, reset in `onReset` at line 233) and pass it as the `max` to `linearScale` so later bursts compress against the original scale and a converged run stays flat at the bottom. Label the domain so the reading is falsifiable — render the domain max at the top of the loss box in the existing `text-[10px] text-caption font-mono` caption style already used at lines 247-249/253-255. Derive the aria-label's step count from `step` rather than `history.length - 1` so it matches the visible counter. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:33`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:225`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:161-165`, `web/src/components/quantum/chart-utils.ts:13-23`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** The kernel explorer's "push the scale high and the boundary starts to alias" caption is false for the `angle` map it offers — accuracy rises instead — The widget prints an unconditional instruction: "Push the scale high and the boundary starts to alias." The GUIDE amplifies it — "switch the feature map and push the scale slider to watch over-encoding *hurt*" — explicitly directing the learner to combine the map selector with the slider. The claim only holds for `iqp`. With the `angle` map, doing exactly what the caption says makes the classifier monotonically *better* across the whole slider range, and the boundary does not gain structure. So a learner who follows the instruction on one of the two offered settings watches the widget's own accuracy readout climb from 68% to 98% while the caption tells them over-encoding is hurting. That is worse than no caption: it teaches a real QML phenomenon (over-encoding / feature-map saturation) with an experiment that refutes it half the time, and the learner cannot tell which half is the lesson. _Fix:_ Make the caption tell the truth for the selected map rather than asserting one map's behaviour for both. Key the sentence off the existing `map` state (already in scope at kernel-explorer.tsx:115) — the aliasing/over-encoding warning for `iqp`, and for `angle` the honest complementary lesson that a plain product feature map keeps improving because its rotation argument never reaches the wrap-around, which is *why* the entangling map is the interesting one. This is copy conditioned on state the component already holds, not new functionality. Pair it with a test that drives the slider under each map and pins the direction of the accuracy readout, closing the coverage gap that hid the contradiction. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:264-267`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:236-245`, `04-quantum-ml/GUIDE.md:171-172`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.test.tsx:17-35`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** The kernel explorer headlines an in-sample accuracy as bare "accuracy", inflating it by 11-13 points against the held-out number — The classifier's predictions are evaluated on the very points that constitute the kernel expansion. Every training point contributes `y_i K(x, x_i)` to its own score with `K(x_i, x_i) = 1` exactly — the maximum possible kernel value, and larger than any other term in the sum — so each point casts a full-strength vote for its own label. The readout presents the resulting number as "quantum-kernel accuracy", with no qualifier, directly above a "linear baseline". In a machine-learning lesson, reporting a training-set score as unqualified accuracy is precisely the error the curriculum should be inoculating against. The repo already knows better: the library's own test for this exact classifier evaluates on a held-out set drawn with a different seed, and the gap between the two numbers is large. _Fix:_ Qualify the labels to match what is computed: render "training accuracy" (or "in-sample accuracy") and "linear baseline (training)" at kernel-explorer.tsx:221-228, and mirror the wording in the `aria-label` at :205. This is a label-only change that makes the widget honest without altering any computation. While there, correct the stale "chance-level on circles/xor" comment at :71 to the measured 68%/53%, so the next reader is not misled about what the baseline represents. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:142-143`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel.ts:43`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:221-228`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/kernel.test.ts:16-22`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** Barren explorer promises the local-cost curve flattens as you raise depth, but its slider caps at a depth where that never happens — and the SVG title hardcodes the opposite claim at every depth — Two pieces of copy make a claim the widget cannot deliver. The callout at `barren-explorer.tsx:286-290` says "Raise the depth slider and even the amber curve flattens (Cerezo 2021)", and 04-quantum-ml/GUIDE.md repeats it: "raise the depth slider and watch even the local cost eventually flatten". The slider's max is 5 (`barren-explorer.tsx:273`), enforced by `parseConfig` at line 51. I ran `gradientVariance` verbatim across depth 1..5 for n=2..8 at the shipped 400 samples: at depth 5 the local variance falls only from 10^-0.65 to 10^-1.02 across the full qubit sweep — a 2.3x drop — against the global curve's 46x. On a log axis auto-scaled to span the global curve's ~3 decades (`loLog`/`hiLog` at lines 118-119), a 0.37-decade tilt is essentially invisible. The learner is told to raise the slider, raises it to max, and sees a curve that still looks like a flat band. Compounding it, the SVG `<title>` at lines 163-167 — the chart's ONLY accessible description via `aria-labelledby` — hardcodes "the amber curve stays in a band at this depth" as static text, so at depth 5 a screen-reader user is told the curve stays flat while the sighted callout tells them to watch it flatten. The two descriptions of the same chart can never both be right. _Fix:_ Make both descriptions match the physics the widget can actually show at depth&lt;=5: reword the callout and the GUIDE line from "flattens" to the measured behavior (the amber band starts to tilt/erode as depth rises, and would collapse at depths this slider deliberately does not reach), and make the `<title>` a function of `deferredDepth` so its claim tracks the curve it is describing instead of asserting "stays in a band" unconditionally. Copy plus one interpolation — no change to the sweep or the slider range. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Accessibility]** Barren's axis tick labels carry the exact inverted gray pair the repo's own caption-contrast guard exists to ban — spelled `fill-` instead of `text-`, so the guard cannot see it — `barren-explorer.tsx:189` (the decade labels) and `:208` (the qubit-count labels) both style real, readable SVG `<text>` with `fill-gray-400 dark:fill-gray-500`. That is precisely the shape `__tests__/caption-contrast.test.ts` was written to block: its docstring at lines 11-16 names the "INVERTED PAIR" — gray-400 on the light surface at 2.49:1 and gray-500 on the dark surface at 4.05:1, "the pair fails at least one theme, usually both". Both numbers are below the 4.5:1 AA floor for text. The guard misses it only because `BASE_GRAY_400` at line 47 and `DARK_EQUAL_OR_DARKER` at line 51 grep `text-gray-*`, and SVG glyphs are colored with `fill-*`. This is not a house-style quibble: the same file gets it RIGHT nineteen lines later — the "qubits n" axis title at line 220 uses `fill-gray-500 dark:fill-gray-400`, the correct light-dark/dark-light orientation, which is also what metrics-explorer, pes-explorer and circuit-diagram use. So barren-explorer disagrees with itself, and the two elements that disagree are the ones a learner must read to know which decade and which qubit count they are looking at. _Fix:_ Flip barren-explorer.tsx:189 and :208 to `fill-gray-500 dark:fill-gray-400`, matching line 220 and the four sibling charts. Then close the guard hole by widening `BASE_GRAY_400`/`BASE_MUTED`/`DARK_EQUAL_OR_DARKER` in caption-contrast.test.ts to accept the `fill-` prefix alongside `text-`, so SVG chart text can never re-acquire the inverted pair (vqe-explorer.tsx:269 carries the same pair and would surface as the one other hit to triage). Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Consistency]** barren-explorer and encoding-explorer still hand-roll the JSON parse preamble that parse-utils single-sources — The WS-6h #67 cross-cutting closure adopted parseJsonObject in 11 widgets, and within F9 vqc-trainer and kernel-explorer use it — but barren-explorer's parseConfig and encoding-explorer's parseSource each re-implement the identical trim/JSON.parse/typeof-object preamble inline. encoding-explorer's copy is behaviorally byte-equivalent to parseJsonObject (same 'invalid JSON' and 'expected a JSON object' strings), so it is pure duplication. barren-explorer additionally uses an optional-fields ParseResult ({config?, error?}) instead of the ok-discriminated union its three siblings use, which forces the defaults 2/300 to be written three separate times (the empty-source return, the undefined-field fallbacks, and the render-side `parsed.config?.depth ?? 2` / `?? 300` re-defaulting) — a real change-one-forget-two drift hazard — and lets a possibly-undefined `parsed.error` reach ErrorCard. _Fix:_ Route both parsers through parseJsonObject: encoding-explorer is a drop-in swap (identical error strings). For barren-explorer, call parseJsonObject then keep its per-field integer/range validation, convert ParseResult to the ok-discriminated union the siblings use, and state the {depth:2, samples:300} defaults exactly once (a DEFAULTS const consumed by both the empty-source path and the field fallbacks), deleting the render-side `?? 2` / `?? 300` re-defaulting. Adjust the one barren test assertion if the invalid-JSON message is unified to 'invalid JSON'. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** One-use ErrorCard wrappers in vqc/kernel and an unreachable 'kernel error' fallback message — Within F9, two of the four widgets (vqc-trainer, kernel-explorer) define a local three-line `ErrorCard` component that only fixes the label and is rendered exactly once, while the other two (barren, encoding) call the shared ErrorCard inline — needless indirection and intra-feature drift. Additionally, kernel-explorer's guard `if (!parsed.ok || !train || !result)` can only fire when `!parsed.ok` (train is null only when parse failed, result null only when train is null), so the `parsed.ok ? "kernel error" : parsed.error` fallback string is unreachable — and if it ever did render it would print the nonsense line 'kernel error: kernel error'. _Fix:_ Inline `<SharedErrorCard label="vqc"|"kernel" message={...} />` at the two call sites and delete the wrappers; in kernel-explorer, keep the compound guard for TypeScript narrowing but pass `message={parsed.ok ? undefined : parsed.error}` (or restructure to early-return on !parsed.ok first) so no fake message string exists. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Consistency]** dj-demo and grover-visualizer percent readouts were missed by the PR #79 formatPercent single-sourcing sweep — PR #79 (3f1c23d) added formatPercent/percentSR to format.ts and routed 32 readouts across 14 widgets through them, naming only currency, SVG geometry, and prose as exclusions — but it never touched dj-demo or grover-visualizer. Both still hand-roll `(x * 100).toFixed(1)}%` in five user-facing readouts (LiveStatus strings, the Bar valueText, the success readout, and the slider aria-valuetext), so the format family the sweep was meant to end still has forked copies inside F8. Output is currently byte-identical (probabilities are non-negative, so no -0 risk), making this pure single-sourcing drift. _Fix:_ Import formatPercent (and percentSR for the LiveStatus/aria-valuetext strings, matching noise-visualizer's SR convention at noise-visualizer.tsx:76,195) in both widgets and replace the five hand-rolled conversions. - [x] **[Correctness]** One of the 30 VQC training points is drawn off the plot with the shipped seed, but still counted in loss/accuracy — makeBlobs(30, 1) clips samples to [-pi, pi], but the scatter viewport only spans PLANE = 3.2, i.e. [-1.6, +1.6]. Executing the shipped generator with its fixed seed shows point x = [1.6298, 0.6045] (class +1) falls outside: featureToSvg(1.6298) = 32.3 &gt; the 32-unit viewBox, so the r=0.7 circle is clipped to an invisible sliver at the right edge. The readout then reports loss/accuracy over 30 points while the learner can only see 29 — 'accuracy 97%' with one misclassified-looking count that cannot be located on the plot, and the decision-boundary heatmap never shows the region that point lives in. _Fix:_ Widen the drawn plane to cover the data (PLANE = 3.4 keeps every shipped-seed point inside with margin, and planeToCell/featureToSvg already derive everything from the constant), or clamp the drawn cx/cy into the viewBox. No math or training change — display geometry only. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:28`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:84-87,133-138`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc.ts:57-67`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Resilience & tests]** Barren explorer runs the full ~150 ms sweep before discarding it on a parse error — The sweep useMemo executes unconditionally (hooks must precede the early return), and on a malformed fence `parsed.config` is undefined so depth/samples fall back to 2/300 via `??` — the widget computes the complete 14-call gradient-variance sweep and then throws the result away to render the error card. The other three F9 widgets pay nothing on their error paths (vqc's data memo is trivial; kernel's result memo short-circuits on `!train` -&gt; null). Only authors hit this path, but it turns every mistyped qbarren fence preview into a main-thread stall for a widget that renders one line of text. _Fix:_ Mirror the kernel explorer's guard: make the memo return an empty Sweep when `!parsed.config` (adding `parsed` to the deps), keeping hook order intact while zeroing the error-path cost. barren-explorer.test.tsx's four error-path tests already cover the rendering contract and would also stop paying the sweep, shaving real time off the jsdom suite. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:84-85`, `web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:96-107`, `web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:109-111`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** One seeded VQC training point renders outside the boundary-plot viewBox and is ~two-thirds clipped — makeBlobs clips features to [-pi, pi] but the plot only spans PLANE = 3.2 ([-1.6, 1.6]). With the fixed seed (makeBlobs(30, 1)) point 28 lands at x = [1.6298, 0.6045]; featureToSvg maps it to cx = 32.30 in a 0..32 viewBox, so its r=0.7 circle is mostly clipped to a sliver at the right edge — deterministically, on every load, for every learner. The accuracy readout counts a point the learner effectively cannot see, undercutting the 'watch the boundary separate the blobs' teaching intent. _Fix:_ Clamp the rendered coordinates into the drawable span (e.g. Math.min(GRID - r, Math.max(r, featureToSvg(v))) for both axes), mirroring makeBlobs' own clip-to-domain move — the point stays visible pinned at the edge, matching the accuracy math. (Widening PLANE to 3.4 also works but re-scales the whole scene for one point.) Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:28,84-87`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/vqc.ts:57-67`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** String-enum config validation is copy-pasted in job-explorer (and three sibling widgets) instead of living in parse-utils beside readNumber — parse-utils.ts exists to single-source the fence-config validation layer (its header says its helpers 'were previously copy-pasted across the hybrid-jobs widgets'), yet the string-enum read — typeof check + allow-list .includes + a 'must be one of' error — is hand-rolled twice in job-explorer and again in kernel-explorer (x2), encoding-explorer, and topology-explorer, and the error copy has already drifted between copies (topology quotes the key and adds a colon; the others do not). _Fix:_ Add readEnum&lt;T extends string&gt;(obj, key, fallback, allowed) to parse-utils.ts mirroring readNumber's {ok,value}|{ok,error} contract, and route the six call sites (job-explorer's provider/instance first) through it so the type check and error copy have one source of truth. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/job-explorer.tsx:77-91`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/parse-utils.ts:44-57`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:54-60`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/topology-explorer.tsx:82-85`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Resilience & tests]** Encoding explorer silently accepts &gt;2-element x arrays and out-of-range values its siblings hard-reject — parseSource in encoding-explorer validates only rawX.length &lt; 2 plus the types of elements 0 and 1, so {"x":[0.6,0.9,"junk"]} passes cleanly — the third element is never type-checked and silently dropped — even though the widget's own error string promises '"x" must be a two-number array' (and the test suite pins that error for [1], making the one-sided check look deliberate when it is not). Separately, in-range values are never validated: {"x":[5,5]} is silently clamped to pi by the clamp at state-init, so a lesson author who typos a feature vector gets a plausible-looking widget at the wrong point with no signal. The sibling F9 parsers follow the opposite convention — barren's parseConfig returns a typed range error ('depth must be an integer in 1..5 (got 9)') and kernel/vqc reject any out-of-contract field value — so a mis-authored qencode fence is the only one in the family that renders wrong instead of erroring. _Fix:_ Tighten parseSource to the contract its error message already states: reject rawX.length !== 2, and validate range the way barren does (either a typed error for |x_i| &gt; pi, or — if the clamp is kept as deliberate UX — validate finiteness/type of exactly two elements and add a comment owning the clamp). Extend the existing exact-error tests with the [1,2,3] and out-of-range cases. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.tsx:61-69`, `web/src/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.tsx:84`, `web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:51-56`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.test.tsx:43-46`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Resilience & tests]** Kernel and barren tests never fire a single control interaction, leaving the deferral contract unpinned — kernel-explorer.test.tsx and barren-explorer.test.tsx are render-plus-parse-error suites only: no test ever changes the kernel scale slider or map select, and none moves the barren depth slider. That leaves both widgets' core code paths — the useDeferredValue recompute, the aria-busy/opacity dimming (the WS-5c pattern both files cite in comments), and the live readout updates on settle — with zero coverage. This is exactly the class of path where this pass's auditors found live defects on the interactive siblings (the frozen VQC loss-curve aria-label, the encoding LiveStatus verbosity), and the two F9 widgets whose entire purpose is slider-driven recompute are the two with no interaction test at all; vqc-trainer.test.tsx (Train click advances the status readout) and encoding-explorer.test.tsx (select change) show the house pattern already exists. _Fix:_ Add one interaction test per widget in the existing files: fireEvent.change on the barren depth slider asserting the variance role=status text changes from its depth-2 value, and on the kernel scale slider (plus a map-select change) asserting the accuracy readout re-renders — pinning the recompute wiring and guarding the aria-busy/readout contract the same way the vqc and encoding suites already do. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Consistency]** encoding-explorer hand-rolls an exact copy of the shared parseJsonObject helper — parse-utils.ts single-sources the fence-body JSON-object parse contract (empty -&gt; defaults, JSON.parse -&gt; "invalid JSON", non-object -&gt; "expected a JSON object") and 11 widgets import it — including the other two JSON-parsing F9 widgets (vqc-trainer, kernel-explorer). encoding-explorer's parseSource re-implements that exact body inline, byte-for-byte down to the error strings, so any future change to the shared parse contract will silently skip this widget. _Fix:_ Replace encoding-explorer.tsx:46-58 with `const base = parseJsonObject(source); if (!base.ok) return base; if (base.obj === null) return { ok: true, value: DEFAULTS };` and keep the bespoke x/encoding field validation on base.obj. Zero rendered-output change; deletes ~13 duplicated lines and re-joins the widget to the shared parse contract. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** barren-explorer's optional-fields ParseResult duplicates the config defaults and is the shape outlier — barren-explorer defines ParseResult as `{ config?: ParsedConfig; error?: string }` while every other F9 widget (and 10+ widgets repo-wide, plus parse-utils itself) uses the discriminated union `{ ok: true, ... } | { ok: false; error }`. The non-discriminated shape forces the component to re-default depth and samples with `?? 2` / `?? 300` — literal duplicates of the defaults already written inside parseConfig — and a compound `parsed.error || !parsed.config` guard. If someone edits the default in parseConfig, the component-side fallback silently diverges in the error-render state. _Fix:_ Convert ParseResult to `{ ok: true; config: ParsedConfig } | { ok: false; error: string }`, hoist DEPTH_DEFAULT/SAMPLES_DEFAULT constants so each default is written once, and simplify the guard to `if (!parsed.ok)`. Keep the bespoke example-shaped invalid-JSON message — it is deliberately pinned by barren-explorer.test.tsx:26-28 and reads better than the generic one — so this is a shape-only refactor, not a parseJsonObject adoption. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Correctness]** VQC loss-curve aria-label miscounts training steps once history is truncated to 240 — The loss curve's accessible name is derived from history.length, but onTrain caps history at MAX_HISTORY=240 entries. After six Train clicks the visible status correctly reads 'step 280' while the curve announces 'Mean-squared-error loss over 239 training steps' — a false statement to screen-reader users (the curve is actually a trailing 239-step window of a 280-step run). Sighted users get no cue either that the x-origin has silently started sliding. _Fix:_ Pass `step` into LossCurve and phrase the label as a window when truncated: 'loss over the last 239 of 280 training steps' (falling back to the current phrasing while history.length - 1 === step). One-line template change; the existing live-region test in vqc-trainer.test.tsx can gain a truncation assertion by clicking Train seven times. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[UI]** Barren log-axis decade labels render '10-4' as flat text — the exponent loses its superscript in the chart (and reads ambiguously in the live readout) — The y-axis tick labels are emitted as `10{d}` inside a single SVG &lt;text&gt; node, so a decade like -4 renders literally as '10-4' at fontSize 7 — visually indistinguishable from a plain number/range, on the one axis that carries the widget's entire claim (exponential 2^-n collapse reads as a straight line only if the axis is understood as log decades). The HTML readout below the chart does superscript correctly with &lt;sup&gt;, so the same quantity is typeset two different ways within one widget; and in that role=status readout the &lt;sup&gt; flattens for screen readers to 'global ≈ 10-3.5', which announces as 'ten minus three point five'. _Fix:_ In the tick label, split the exponent into a shifted tspan: `10<tspan dy={-2.5} fontSize={5}>{d}</tspan>` (and reset dy is unneeded since it's the last node) — matching the readout's superscript typography. For the readout's SR phrasing, wrap the paragraph's announcement value as '10^-3.5' via an sr-only alternative or reword to 'about 1e-3.5', keeping the visible &lt;sup&gt; rendering unchanged. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[UI]** F9 widgets never finished the Instrument token migration: vqc-trainer readouts sit on cool Tailwind grays and the kernel select drops the field fill/ink the sibling select uses — PR #172's ledger entry records this exact drift class for the shell ('Sidebar and TOC never finished the PR #169 token migration - cool grays...') and swept it — but inside F9 the same pre-redesign values remain. vqc-trainer's primary status line uses text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-100 and its description text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400 (cool slate hues) while its direct sibling kernel-explorer renders the equivalent lines with the warm text-(--ink)/text-caption tokens on the same warm glass surface; vqc's LossCurve panel is fill-gray-50 dark:fill-gray-900/40 rather than the --field/--track surface family. And the two F9 &lt;select&gt; controls diverge: kernel-explorer's map select is border-(--bd) bg-transparent with no text token, while encoding-explorer's (and dj-demo's) select is the tokenized bg-(--field) text-(--ink) recipe — two visibly different dropdown fills within the same lesson track. _Fix:_ Finish the sweep inside F9: vqc-trainer readout → text-(--ink), description → text-caption, LossCurve backdrop → the --field/--track fill (via a fill-[var(--track)] style or class); kernel-explorer select → the encoding-explorer recipe (bg-(--field) text-(--ink)). Every replacement token already exists and is used lines away; zero new CSS. (The same gray-readout pattern also survives in vqe-explorer.tsx:324 and param-compile-explorer.tsx:241 — worth folding into the same sweep, though those belong to F10/F11.) Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** vqc.ts threads a dead `scale` parameter through four exported functions that no caller or test ever sets — `vqcOutput`, `paramShiftGrad`, `mseLoss` and `trainStep` all carry a trailing `scale = 1` parameter, and `trainStep` and `paramShiftGrad` exist only to forward it down the chain. vqc-trainer.tsx is the module's sole production consumer and never passes it; vqc.test.ts never passes it either. It is a feature-map knob (the counterpart of kernel-explorer's live `scale` slider) that was never wired into the VQC widget, so today it is a permanently-1 argument widening four public signatures. It carries a live hazard: because it is the last positional parameter on `trainStep(data, theta, bias, lr, scale)` but the *fifth* on `paramShiftGrad(x, theta, bias, j, scale)`, a future caller adding an argument in the wrong slot silently rescales the feature encoding rather than erroring. _Fix:_ Delete the `scale` parameter from all four vqc.ts signatures and inline `ry(x[0])` / `ry(x[1])`. Nothing needs to change at any call site since every one already relies on the default. If the intent was ever to give VqcTrainer kernel-explorer's scale slider, that is a feature decision, not something a dead default should hold open — record it separately rather than keeping the parameter alive unexercised. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqc.ts:14`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc.ts:31,37,44`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:61,204,211,219,233`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/vqc.test.ts:15-23`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Architecture]** vqc-trainer keeps pure model functions in the view file, outside the tested vqc.ts boundary its sibling kernel.ts establishes — Feature 9 has a clean convention: the physics/ML math lives in a plain .ts module (vqc.ts, kernel.ts, barren.ts, encoding.ts) with a dedicated unit test, and the .tsx file is presentation. vqc-trainer breaks it in one place — `accuracyOf` and `initTheta` are pure model functions defined in the component file. `accuracyOf` computes the headline number the learner reads ("accuracy 93%"), runs the same forward pass as `mseLoss`, and belongs beside it; its sibling kernel.ts exports exactly this as `accuracy` and kernel.test.ts asserts on it. Because it sits in the .tsx, it has no unit test — vqc.test.ts covers expectZ0, paramShiftGrad, mseLoss, trainStep and makeBlobs but cannot reach it, and vqc-trainer.test.tsx only asserts the step counter advances. The result is that the single most visible output of the widget is the only piece of its math with no direct coverage. _Fix:_ Move `accuracyOf` into vqc.ts next to `mseLoss` (it shares the same `vqcOutput` forward pass and the same `Point[]` input), and move `initTheta` there too so the widget's initialization policy sits with the parameter count constant `N_PARAMS` that defines its length. Add two assertions to vqc.test.ts: accuracy is 1.0 for a hand-fitted theta on the separable blobs, and initTheta returns N_PARAMS values inside the documented [-0.1, 0.3] band. vqc-trainer.tsx then imports both alongside the functions it already pulls from vqc.ts, and the view file drops to presentation only. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** Sixteen widgets each carry a three-line ErrorCard shim whose only job is to bind a label, plus an `ErrorCard as SharedErrorCard` import rename — A mechanical adoption pass left a permanent indirection layer: sixteen widgets import the shared component under an alias, then define a private wrapper that re-exposes it with the label pre-bound. Every one of these is the same three lines with one string changed. The alias also means a reader of any of these files sees `<ErrorCard message={...} />` and has to scroll up to learn it is not the shared component with that name. The eight widgets that call the shared component directly with `label=` prove the shim buys nothing. _Fix:_ Drop the sixteen wrappers and the sixteen `as SharedErrorCard` renames; inline `label=` at each of the ~16 call sites (each widget has exactly one or two). Net removal of ~48 lines and the shadowed name, and `ErrorCard` then means one thing everywhere in the directory. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:79-81`, `web/src/components/quantum/metrics-explorer.tsx:88-90`, `web/src/components/quantum/cost-estimate-widget.tsx:112`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** Deferred-recompute staleness is signalled on the SVG only — the chip, the status readout, and the kernel's aria-label all keep asserting values the picture does not show — Both kernel and barren use useDeferredValue and correctly mark the SVG `aria-busy` + `opacity-60` while it lags. But the other surfaces that render the SAME deferred data carry no staleness signal at all, and one actively lies. The kernel SVG's aria-label interpolates the IMMEDIATE `scale` while the cells it describes were computed from `deferredScale`, so a screen-reader user reading the image gets a number the image does not depict. The accuracy live region renders deferred `result.acc` with no aria-busy, so a sighted user sees a crisp accuracy sitting next to a dimmed boundary with no cue that the number is stale too — and a polite live region will announce the intermediate value. Barren has the same shape: the header chip shows the immediate depth next to a dimmed plot computed at the previous depth. _Fix:_ Hoist the existing staleness flag one level in each widget (`const stale = scale !== deferredScale` / `depth !== deferredDepth`) and apply it to every surface that renders deferred data, not just the SVG: build the kernel SVG's aria-label from `deferredScale` so it describes what is actually drawn, and put `aria-busy={stale}` plus the same `opacity-60 transition-opacity` on the accuracy status block and on barren's readout, so the polite regions hold their announcement until the value settles instead of narrating intermediate states. Barren's `<Chip>depth = ...</Chip>` should likewise read `deferredDepth` so the header never labels a plot it does not describe. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:205`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:137`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:206-207`, `web/src/components/quantum/kernel-explorer.tsx:220-228`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** VQC trainer's only heading is sr-only, sits after both plots in DOM order, and its generated id is dead — The widget generates a heading id, stamps it on a visually-hidden h3, and then never references it from anything — no aria-labelledby consumes it, so the useId call and the id attribute are both inert. Separately, that h3 is nested inside the second flex column, which means a screen-reader user navigating by heading reaches the widget's name only AFTER passing its two role="img" plots — the plots announce before anything says what widget they belong to. Meanwhile the card's visible "VQC" eyebrow stays a plain span rather than being promoted to the heading, even though WidgetCard already supports exactly that and another explorable in the repo already does it. _Fix:_ Drop the mid-card sr-only h3 and adopt the checkpoint-explorer pattern: pass `eyebrowAs="h3"` on the WidgetCard so the visible "VQC" eyebrow becomes the heading and lands first in DOM order, ahead of both plots. If the longer accessible name is still wanted, carry it as an sr-only span inside the eyebrow rather than as a second, unreferenced heading — and remove the now-unused `useId` at line 205 rather than leaving a generated id nothing points at. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Resilience & tests]** The barren explorer runs its full ~100 ms sweep before the parse-error early-return, so a malformed fence pays for a chart it never draws — Hook ordering forces the sweep useMemo to execute before the component can bail out on a parse failure, and nothing inside the memo guards on the parse result. `initDepth` and `samples` fall back to 2 and 300 via `?? `, so an unparseable or out-of-range source still drives a complete seven-qubit-count, two-cost, 300-sample sweep — the heaviest computation in the feature — and only then renders an error card. The result is thrown away and the memo is retained for the component's lifetime. This is the mirror image of the already-recorded VQC item (where cheap work sits *after* the early return and was correctly skipped as not worth it): here it is the expensive work sitting *before* it, and unlike the VQC case the guard is free because the error return at :109 precedes every consumer of the sweep. _Fix:_ Add a first-line guard inside the memo body — `if (parsed.error || !parsed.config) return { ns: [], global: [], local: [] };` — and include `parsed` in the dependency array. The hook still runs in the same order (no rules-of-hooks violation), the early return at :109 fires before anything reads the empty arrays, and the error path becomes free. Same one-line shape works for any other widget in the family that computes before validating. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:96-107`, `web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:85-86`, `web/src/components/quantum/barren-explorer.tsx:109-111`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/barren-explorer.test.tsx:26-41`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Cleaner code]** Encoding explorer reports the state norm on four surfaces, including inside both sliders' aria-valuetext — and it is provably pinned at 1.000 for every reachable input — `encoding-explorer.tsx:107-110` recomputes `norm` on every state change, and the value is then rendered in four places: the `LiveStatus` sentence (line 124), the dedicated `‖ψ‖ = ...` readout (lines 214-216), and — the costly ones — appended to BOTH sliders' `ariaValueText` at lines 182 and 198. All three encodings are norm-preserving by construction: angle and IQP are unitary circuits on `zeroState(2)`, and `amplitudeState` divides by its own norm at encoding.ts:22 (with a `|0...0>` fallback below 1e-9, so even the both-sliders-at-zero case is safe). I swept the entire reachable slider grid — 61 x 61 positions across `[-π, π]` at the shipped `step={PI/60}`, for angle and IQP — and the worst deviation is |norm - 1| = 8.9e-16. `norm.toFixed(3)` is therefore the literal string "1.000" in every configuration the widget can ever be in. The consequence is worst on the sliders: a keyboard user arrowing x0 hears "...norm 1.000" appended to every single tick, padding the announcement of the one number that is actually changing with a constant that cannot change. _Fix:_ Strip the invariant from the two `ariaValueText` strings so each slider announces only its own feature value (the thing that moves), and drop it from the LiveStatus sentence. Keep the single visible `‖ψ‖ = 1.000` line if it is doing pedagogical work — it teaches that feature maps preserve normalization — but it should be stated once, not re-announced on every tick as if it were a live measurement. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.tsx:107-110`, `web/src/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.tsx:182`, `web/src/components/quantum/encoding-explorer.tsx:124`, `web/src/components/quantum/encoding.ts:22`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** vqc-trainer is the only one of the four QML widgets still on pre-Instrument raw Tailwind grays — the #169/#172 token sweep skipped it — kernel-explorer.tsx and encoding-explorer.tsx contain ZERO `text-gray-*` classes; they route every text tier through the Instrument tokens (`text-(--ink)`, `text-caption`, `text-(--mut)`) introduced by the #169 restyle and hardened by the #172 token sweep. barren-explorer's single `text-gray-*` is a gridline stroke, not text. vqc-trainer.tsx is the outlier: its primary status readout at line 269 uses `text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-100` and its explainer at line 294 uses `text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400` — the hand-rolled predecessor of the shared `.text-caption` utility that caption-contrast.test.ts's first assertion exists to pin. This is visible, not theoretical: the qkernel and qvqc fences sit nine lines apart in 04-quantum-ml/GUIDE.md (lines 174 and 183), so the two status readouts render adjacent on the page — one in the warm `--ink` (oklch(0.23 0.008 82) light / oklch(0.96 0.004 88) dark) and one in Tailwind's cool blue-gray-800. It also measures dimmer: `--mut` is documented at 6.5:1 on the light surface, while gray-500 is 4.6:1, so the VQC explainer is a visibly weaker caption than every sibling caption on the same page. The line-294 pair passes the caption-contrast grep (it is neither inverted nor theme-blind), which is exactly why the sweep left it behind. _Fix:_ Swap vqc-trainer.tsx:269 to `text-(--ink)` and :294 to `text-caption`, matching kernel-explorer.tsx:221 and :264. Two class edits bring the last QML widget onto the token system and put its readout in the same ink as the widget directly above it in the lesson. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:269`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:294`, `web/src/app/globals.css:170-171,225-226`, `web/__tests__/caption-contrast.test.ts:65-75`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** VQC's boundary plot hardcodes a feature span narrower than the data it is drawing, clipping a training point at the SVG edge — `vqc-trainer.tsx:28` fixes `PLANE = 3.2`, so the plot covers [-1.6, +1.6] in both axes, and `featureToSvg` (lines 84-87) maps that window onto the 0..GRID viewBox. But `makeBlobs` in vqc.ts:52-60 draws from N(±0.7, 0.35) and clips only to [-π, π] — a range more than twice as wide as the plot. The two extents are unrelated constants, and with the shipped seed (`makeBlobs(30, 1)` at line 200) they collide: one of the 30 points lands at x0 = 1.630, past the 1.6 half-plane. `featureToSvg(1.630)` = 32.3 against a `viewBox="0 0 32 32"`, so with r = 0.7 that circle spans 31.6..33.0 and the SVG root's default overflow clip renders it as a sliver glued to the right edge. The accuracy readout at line 212 counts all 30 points including the one the learner cannot see, so the picture and the number are drawn from different populations. Note the sibling kernel explorer does not have this problem — I checked its `SPAN = 1.1` against `makeDataset('circles'|'xor', 60, 1)` and the data maxes at 1.084, fully inside the plot. _Fix:_ Derive the plot window from the data instead of hardcoding it — size PLANE from the actual extent of `data` (with a small margin) in the same `useMemo` that already builds the blobs at line 200, so the scatter always contains every point the accuracy figure counts. If the fixed window is deliberate, clamp `featureToSvg` so an out-of-range point pins visibly at the edge rather than being silently clipped. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:27-28`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc-trainer.tsx:84-87`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqc.ts:52-60`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)

### 10. Quantum chemistry explorables

> **Inline verification pass — 2026-06-20** (verify stage server-throttled; direct source review). _Current state:_ the chemistry math is correct and the PES a11y readout the prior pass flagged is already fixed; remaining items are VQE plot polish (rubber-banding, axis scale), DRY (ErrorCard ×4, hand-rolled Bloch), and JW cell contrast.

- [x] **[Accessibility]** CLEARED 2026-06-20 (prior claim refuted — already fixed) — **PES scrubber already announces the energies**. The prior item said the bond-length scrubber announces only the length via `aria-valuetext`; in current code `aria-valuetext` already embeds FCI, Hartree-Fock, and the gap. No action. (`pes-explorer.tsx:371` — aria-valuetext reads "R angstrom; FCI …, Hartree-Fock …, gap … hartree".)
- [x] **[UI]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-5c: dropped CSS transitions on marker/dropline; added theta x-axis ticks; floor label anchors the energy min) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **VQE optimizer trace rubber-bands and the plot has no axis scale**. The optimizer animates with `setTimeout(tick, ANIM_MS=32)` (~31 fps) while the trace marker and lines use CSS `transition-[cx,cy] duration-150`, so each 32ms position update restarts a 150ms ease the next frame interrupts — the marker visibly lags/rubber-bands during Optimize. The energy/θ plot also has only caption text, no numeric axis ticks/scale, so values are unanchored. _Fix:_ drop the CSS transition during animation (let the 32ms steps drive motion) or raise `ANIM_MS` to ≥150ms; add min/mid/max axis ticks. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`vqe-explorer.tsx:33` ANIM_MS=32 · `:192,199` setTimeout(tick, ANIM_MS) · `:314,372,379` markers/lines use `transition-... duration-150` · no axis-tick text in the plot) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Consistency & reuse]** DONE 2026-06-21 (shared `ErrorCard` in #36 WS-2a; VQE now uses the shared `BlochDial` at size=86 — #-WS2-VQE, browser-verified) — ✓verified — confirms prior (refined) — **ErrorCard duplicated across all four chemistry widgets; VQE hand-rolls a Bloch indicator**. `function ErrorCard` is defined separately in `vqe-explorer.tsx`, `pes-explorer.tsx`, `jw-explorer.tsx`, and `hamiltonian-explorer.tsx` (the same scaffold also recurs in the algorithms/hybrid widgets — a suite-wide extraction candidate). VQE draws its own X–Z state indicator with hand-rolled SVG rather than reusing the shared `BlochDial`. _Refinement of the prior claim:_ PES does NOT duplicate a `chemistry.ts` interpolation — it interpolates the precomputed `h2-data` curve locally; `chemistry.ts` exposes `exactGround`/`vqeEnergy`, not an interp helper, so the real duplication is the local interpolation + bond-length lerp and the ErrorCard scaffold, not an ignored chemistry.ts function. _Fix:_ extract a shared `ErrorCard` and reuse `BlochDial` in VQE. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`ErrorCard` in vqe-explorer.tsx / pes-explorer.tsx / jw-explorer.tsx / hamiltonian-explorer.tsx · vqe-explorer.tsx hand-rolled SVG indicator, no BlochDial import · `pes-explorer.tsx:5` imports only the `H2Point` type from chemistry.ts) — added 2026-06-20
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-21 (WS-3) — ✓verified — confirms prior — **Low-contrast JW cell labels fall below WCAG AA**. The Jordan–Wigner identity/qubit cell labels render in `text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500` (≈2.5:1 on white), below the 4.5:1 AA floor for normal text. _Fix:_ bump these to the `text-gray-500/600` used by the readout text. (The Hamiltonian explorer mostly uses `text-gray-500/600` already; the JW Pauli X/Y glyph-overflow sub-claim was not separately reproduced and is carried.) Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`jw-explorer.tsx:117,218,223,333` — `text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500` on identity/qubit cells) — added 2026-06-20

_New (2026-06-25 deep audit):_
- [x] **[UI/UX · teaching correctness]** DONE 2026-06-25 (WS-6a, PR #60 merged) — **The VQE energy landscape is drawn upside-down — the variational floor/minimum renders at the TOP of the SVG.** `energyToY = padY + ((e - eMin)/span)*plotH` maps the minimum to `y=padY` (top), so on Optimize the marker visibly *climbs* to reach the "floor," directly contradicting the widget's own copy ("slides θ down to the variational floor") and the sibling PES plot, which correctly puts lower energy lower. Flip the mapping to `energyToY = padY + ((eMax - e)/span)*plotH` (matching `pes-explorer.tsx:112` `sy`); the floor line, marker, drop-line and ticks all derive from it and follow automatically. - [x] **[UI/UX · teaching correctness]** DONE 2026-06-25 (WS-6a, PR #60 merged) — **JW explorer's note asserts a phantom Z-string for mode 0 — the default view.** The note always claims "The trailing Z-string counts the parity of every lower-index orbital (q0 through q{…})"; for `DEFAULT_CONFIG.mode = 0` it renders "q0 through q0", describing a Z on q0 that doesn't exist (q0 carries the X/Y factor). The inline formula directly above already hides the Z-string span when the chain is empty, so the prose contradicts the diagram on first render. Branch the paragraph on `image.zChain.length > 0` (as the inline span already is). Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`jw-explorer.tsx:34-39,304-309,342-349`; `jw.ts:51`) — added 2026-06-25

> **2026-07-19 Category B re-audit** — 39 adversarially-verified findings (independent skeptic per claim, default-refute; correctness claims re-derived from first principles, one by porting the shipped code to a measurement harness).

- [x] **[Correctness]** VQE Optimize animation drags the marker uphill over the energy maximum for ~46% of starting angles — onOptimize wraps the gradient-descent result into [-pi, pi] via atan2 and then lerps theta linearly from the start angle to that WRAPPED angle. The fixture's optimum sits near theta* = -2.912 rad and the energy maximum near +0.230 rad (verified by replicating the code path against the committed fixture at R=0.75), so for any start theta in (0.23, pi] real GD flows rightward to the unwrapped minimum at +3.371, but the animation instead sweeps leftward across the plot THROUGH the global maximum. From start theta=3.0 the marker climbs 1.542 Ha - 96.6% of the plot height - up the hill and back down; from theta=2.0 it climbs 59.8%; from theta=1.0, 14.1%. Even the default start 0.4 rises slightly. The caption directly below says Optimize 'runs parameter-shift gradient descent ... and slides theta down to the variational floor' - gradient descent never ascends, so the widget's flagship interaction teaches a wrong mental model of the optimizer whenever the learner explores starts right of the maximum. _Fix:_ Lerp toward the RAW gradient-descent endpoint (result.theta[0]) and wrap each frame for display: next = start + (result.theta[0] - start) * t; setTheta(Math.atan2(Math.sin(next), Math.cos(next))). GD's endpoint is downhill-connected to the start within one basin, so energy is monotone non-increasing along this path; the marker exits the +pi edge and re-enters at -pi (the same point on the periodic curve) instead of climbing the barrier. The existing 'keeps theta within [-pi, pi] after Optimize' test stays green since every displayed frame is wrapped. Pin it with a fake-timer test: set the slider to 3.0, click Optimize, and assert energy1q at each animation frame never exceeds the previous frame by more than epsilon. Impact: High. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:162`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:180`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:372-377`, `web/src/components/quantum/chemistry.ts:255-277`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** qham's TermBars is a hand-rolled copy of the shared Bar row that missed the --track token migration and re-implements reduced-motion in JS — hamiltonian-explorer.tsx carries a private TermBars renderer that duplicates the shared Bar's exact geometry (w-12 mono label, h-3 flex-1 rounded-full track, absolute fill, right-aligned tabular value) but with an opaque legacy track color, while every Bar-based widget was moved to the translucent --track token in the Instrument redesign — so qham's bars visibly differ from every other magnitude bar on the site. It also threads a usePrefersReducedMotion() hook into an `animate` prop whose only effect is dropping a transition class that ALREADY contains motion-reduce:transition-none, i.e. the JS gate is fully redundant with its own CSS guard (the shared Bar relies on the CSS guard alone). _Fix:_ Minimum: switch TermBars' track to `bg-(--track)` and delete the redundant reduced-motion plumbing (always emit the transition classes; drop the usePrefersReducedMotion import, `reduced`, and the `animate` prop). Better: relax Bar's single blocker — the hardcoded `|{label}⟩` ket wrapper (widget-ui.tsx:102-104) — with a raw-label option, then render each term via Bar (fillClass carries the signed accent-mix fill, valueWidth="w-16"), deleting the ~40-line hand-roll the same way WS-6c consolidated the other bar copies. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:117-159`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:145`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:178,211`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:106-111`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** Shared engine text/field defaults still carry pre-Instrument cool-gray literals, and callers have re-drifted by overriding them with --mut — widget-ui.tsx's neutral palette predates the Instrument token system: LabeledSlider's default label is text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300 and its value readout is text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400; StateReadout body is text-gray-700 dark:text-gray-200; ErrorCard is text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400; secondaryActionClass/fieldClass use gray-200/gray-50/gray-900 borders and fills. Tailwind grays are cool (hue ~247-264) while the Instrument neutrals (--ink/--mut, hue 82-88) are warm, so one card mixes two gray families — e.g. CircuitLab renders its slider value in cool gray-500 directly under Bar readouts in warm --mut. Worse, the centralization has re-fragmented: 20+ LabeledSlider callers now override labelClassName with text-(--mut) while vqe-explorer and qaoa-explorer still render the stale literal, so sibling widgets' slider labels disagree — exactly the drift LabeledSlider was extracted to end. _Fix:_ Move the engine defaults onto the Instrument tokens — labelClassName default text-(--mut), value span text-caption, StateReadout body text-(--ink) (keeping the accent Dirac span), ErrorCard text-caption, secondaryActionClass/fieldClass on border-(--bd)/bg-(--field) — then delete the now-redundant per-caller labelClassName color overrides. token-contrast.test.ts already guarantees the token tiers, so the swap is contrast-safe by construction. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:310`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:353-359`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:159,259,274-281`, `web/src/components/quantum/circuit-lab.tsx:68`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Architecture]** qham drives one slider through two different fixture-sampling conventions, and its "sampled at R" caption plus provenance note only hold for one of them — HamiltonianExplorer resolves the bond length twice, by two incompatible rules. The full 15-term view snaps to the nearest committed fixture point (`nearestPoint`), while the tapered 1-qubit view linearly interpolates between fixture points (`h2OneQubit`). One `R` slider drives both. Every other chemistry widget uses interpolation only, so qham is the lone holder of a second convention — and it keeps a private `nearestPoint` helper that exists nowhere else in the feature. The user-visible consequence is on the shipped lesson: the live fence body is `{ "R": 0.74 }`, and the fixture grid is 0.30 + 0.05k, so 0.74 is off-grid. On first paint the widget shows the slider reading "0.74 Å" next to a caption reading "sampled at R = 0.75 Å", and after toggling to tapered mode the caption still claims a sampling point whose coefficients are not the ones displayed (those are interpolated at 0.74). The footer additionally asserts coefficients "are read directly from the committed fixture ... none are invented", which is true of the 15-term path but not of the interpolated tapered path. I am reporting the convention split and the caption/provenance coupling, not a numerical error in either function. _Fix:_ Pick one sampling convention for the widget and make the surrounding copy follow it. Simplest coherent option: keep `nearestPoint` only if the caption and provenance line are made mode-aware, so tapered mode says "interpolated at R = &lt;slider R&gt;" and drops the "read directly ... none are invented" claim for that branch; or delete `nearestPoint` entirely and let the full-term list interpolate `point.jw` the way every sibling widget interpolates, which removes the private helper and makes the one caption true in both modes. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (`web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:181`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:192`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:284`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:76`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** qham tapering toggle: role="switch" plus a flipping action-verb name announces contradictory state — The tapering control is role="switch" with aria-checked={tapered}, but its accessible name is the NEXT action, not the thing switched: when tapering is ON the name becomes "Show full 4-qubit Hamiltonian" while the state announces "on" — a screen-reader user hears "Show full 4-qubit Hamiltonian, switch, on" and can reasonably conclude the full 4-qubit view is the active one (it is not). Switch/toggle semantics require a stable name with state carried by aria-checked; an action label that inverts per state contradicts it. This is original #10 markup that every a11y pass (WS-3, WS-6b, WS-B1) left untouched. _Fix:_ Drop role="switch"/aria-checked and leave it a plain action button — the flip-label "do the other thing" pattern is self-describing, and the chips + LiveStatus already announce the resulting mode. (Alternative: keep switch semantics with a stable visible label like "Z2 symmetry tapering", but that is a larger visual change.) Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:273-280`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:227`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.test.tsx:45`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** qvqe theta slider double-announces the energy on every step (valuetext + LiveStatus overlap) — The theta slider's aria-valuetext ends with "energy N hartree" (original #10 markup) and WS-6b (#62) later layered a LiveStatus whose text also leads with "Energy N hartree..." and changes on every theta tick. A keyboard/SR user scrubbing theta therefore hears the energy twice per arrow press — once immediately via valuetext, once via the polite status. This violates the project's own documented live-region discipline: the qham slider comment explicitly avoids repeating a value across valuetext and LiveStatus because it "would double-announce the same label + coefficient". _Fix:_ Make the two channels complementary as qham does: trim the slider valuetext to the angle plus the gap-above-floor (the datum LiveStatus does not lead with), or keep valuetext angle-only and let LiveStatus own the energy. One string change either way; vqe-explorer.test.tsx:70 pins only that the status contains /hartree/i. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:347`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:212-216`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:250-254`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UX]** qham tapered mode shows interpolated coefficients under a caption and honesty note claiming they are sampled directly from the fixture — In full mode the 15 terms come from the nearest fixture point and the caption "sampled at R = …" correctly discloses the snap. In tapered mode the displayed c0/cz/cx come from h2OneQubit(R), which linearly interpolates between fixture points — yet the same caption still names the nearest sample, and the provenance footer asserts "Coefficients are read directly from the committed H2 fixture … none are invented." This is the shipped default state: the live lesson fence passes R=0.74 (off the fixture's 0.05 Å grid), so one click of the tapering toggle shows interpolated c0 = −0.3262 Ha captioned as "sampled at R = 0.75 Å", whose actual sample is c0 = −0.3387 Ha — a 12.5 mHa discrepancy in a widget whose stated value proposition is provenance honesty. _Fix:_ Drive tapered mode from the same nearest sample as full mode — use point.c0/point.cz/point.cx instead of h2OneQubit(R) — so the caption, the honesty note, and the slider's aria-valuetext (hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:255-265) all become literally true and the two modes share one sampling story. (Alternatively, switch the caption to "interpolated at R = …" when tapered, but that leaves the "none are invented" footer strained.) The existing aria-valuetext test only pattern-matches the coefficient format, so no pinned behavior changes. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:192`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:181,282-285`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:317-321`, `05-quantum-chemistry/GUIDE.md:110-112`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** VQE double-announces the energy on every slider step — the exact live-region collision the sibling qham widget was deliberately fixed to avoid — `vqe-explorer.tsx` puts the same energy value in two competing announcement channels. The polite LiveStatus renders `Energy {hartreeSR(energy)}, {hartreeSR(aboveFloor)} above the exact ground floor {hartreeSR(floor)}` (line 212-216), and the θ slider's `ariaValueText` renders `{theta} radians, energy {hartreeSR(energy)}` (line 347). Both strings change on every arrow-key press, so a keyboard screen-reader user hears the value text and then the re-fired status region for a single keystroke. The campaign already identified and resolved precisely this hazard in the sibling Hamiltonian explorer, and wrote the reasoning into the code and into a regression test. _Fix:_ Apply the qham resolution to qvqe: make the θ slider's `ariaValueText` carry only the angle (`${formatFixed(theta,2)} radians`) and leave the energy/gap/floor to the LiveStatus, which is also the only channel that covers the non-slider Optimize animation. Extend `vqe-explorer.test.tsx` with the mirror of the qham assertion (slider valuetext must not match /hartree/) so the two chemistry sliders stay under one rule. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:212-216`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:347`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:250-255`, `web/__tests__/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.test.tsx:75-82`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Resilience / Teaching correctness]** loadH2Curve validates every field except `equilibrium` — the one block PES prints as four teaching facts and all three widgets open on — and the "source of truth" test block never compares it to the actual curve minimum — The fixture loader's docstring promises it "throws on shape mismatch", and it does check basis, jwTerms, per-point finiteness, jw row length and monotonic R — but it never touches `equilibrium` or `molecule`. Nothing in the loader or the test suite ties `equilibrium.{R,fci,hf}` to the actual argmin of `points`. PES draws its amber equilibrium dot from a coordinate pair sourced independently of the FCI path it is supposed to mark, and prints equilibrium R / min FCI / well depth to four decimals; qham and qvqe both default their bond length to `H.equilibrium.R`. A fixture regen on a different grid moves all of that with zero signal — and a regen that drops the block throws at render time on a page with no error boundary at all. _Fix:_ Extend the guard that already exists in `loadH2Curve` (chemistry.ts:303-327) rather than adding a new mechanism: assert `equilibrium` is present with finite R/fci/hf, that `equilibrium.R` matches one of the sampled `points[i].R`, and that `equilibrium.fci` equals the minimum `fci` over `points` (with `hf` its partner) to the fixture's own precision — throwing the same "loadH2Curve: malformed fixture" error it already throws. Add the matching case to the existing "H2 fixture (the source of truth)" describe block so a regen that moves the minimum fails at test time instead of silently repainting PES's four teaching numbers and shifting the amber dot off its own curve. While there, source qham's provenance sentence from `H.provenance` instead of the duplicated literal. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/chemistry.ts:302-327`, `web/src/components/quantum/pes-explorer.tsx:96-97`, `web/src/components/quantum/pes-explorer.tsx:364-379`, `web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:41`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** qham's TermBars re-implements the shared Bar row and drifts off the --track token, putting an opaque bar track on a glass card — `TermBars` is a hand-rolled copy of the shared `Bar` primitive: same w-12 mono label column, same `relative h-3 flex-1 rounded-full` track, same `absolute inset-y-0 left-0 rounded-full` fill sized by `pct.toFixed(2)`, same right-aligned `tabular-nums` value column. The copy has drifted on the surface token — it paints the track `bg-gray-100 dark:bg-gray-800` (fully opaque) while the shared Bar and every other bar-like track in the widget family use `bg-(--track)`, a deliberately translucent token (alpha 0.10 light / 0.06 dark) chosen to read correctly through the `.glass` card shell. qham is the only bar track in the quantum widget set still painting opaque gray, so its 15 rows sit as solid blocks on an otherwise translucent card. The copy also silently lacks the shared row's `bar-shimmer` treatment. Full reuse is currently blocked because `Bar` hardcodes a ket wrapper around its label (`|{label}⟩`), which is wrong for Pauli strings like `IIIZ` — that hardcoding is the actual reason the copy exists. _Fix:_ Give `Bar` an escape hatch for its label (e.g. an optional `labelNode`, or a `ket = true` flag callers can turn off) and delete `TermBars` in favour of the shared row, passing `fillClass` for the negative-coefficient tone. If that refactor is deferred, at minimum swap the hand-rolled track to `bg-(--track)` so the qham bars stop being the only opaque tracks on a glass card. Impact: Low. Effort: Medium. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** qham labels its term list through a zero-size absolutely-positioned SVG instead of the repo's sr-only paragraph idiom — To give the bar list an accessible summary, hamiltonian-explorer renders a 0x0 `<svg role="img" aria-label className="absolute">` containing a `<title>` — the only occurrence of this trick in the codebase (it dates to the original #10 commit, before the widget family settled on a simpler idiom). Later widgets announce the same kind of summary with a plain `<p className="sr-only">`, which is less DOM, needs no positioning hack, and is not at risk of zero-size elements being skipped by AT. _Fix:_ Replace the zero-size SVG with `<p className="sr-only">{listLabel}</p>` — identical announcement, matches the job-explorer precedent, removes the phantom svg node and its `absolute` positioning. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/hamiltonian-explorer.tsx:296-304`, `web/src/components/quantum/job-explorer.tsx:203`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** The three fixture-range parsers (qvqe/qham/qpes) hand-roll the same optional-number-in-range validation with drifting validators and unit spelling — All three widgets parse an optional bond-length field against the fixture's R range, each with its own copy: qham checks only Number.isNaN (vqe/pes check !Number.isFinite) and says '"R" must be a number'; vqe says 'must be a finite number (angstrom)'; pes says 'must be a finite number'. qham also capitalizes the unit — error text '... Angstrom' and slider ariaLabel 'in Angstrom' — while vqe/pes and format.ts's angstromSR use lowercase, a convention qham's own test suite explicitly pins ('uses lowercase screen-reader units... never a capitalized unit') but whose regex only inspects number-prefixed textContent, so the aria-label and error string escape it. parse-utils.ts already exists as the single-source home for exactly this kind of fence-body reader (readNumber clamps; these three need the erroring variant). _Fix:_ Add a `readNumberInRange(obj, key, fallback, lo, hi)` beside readNumber in parse-utils.ts (errors instead of clamping, lowercase unit passed by the caller) and adopt it in all three parseSource functions; fix qham's two 'Angstrom' strings to lowercase to match format.ts and the pinned convention. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Accessibility]** qham exposes its term-list summary through a 0x0 SVG, which AT engines may prune — The overall Hamiltonian summary ("Four-qubit Jordan-Wigner H2 Hamiltonian: 15 weighted Pauli terms at bond length..., largest magnitude...") is attached to a phantom &lt;svg width={0} height={0} role="img"&gt; rather than to visible content or an sr-only element. Zero-area elements are pruned from the accessibility tree by some engines (WebKit/VoiceOver in particular prunes zero-size graphics), so the summary — the only place the term COUNT and largest magnitude are stated for AT in full-mode — may silently never be announced. The repo's own idiom for off-screen text is the .sr-only clip pattern (which keeps a 1px box precisely to stay exposed), used by LiveStatus and BlochVectorSR. _Fix:_ Replace the 0x0 SVG with a &lt;p className="sr-only"&gt;{listLabel}&lt;/p&gt; (or fold listLabel into the existing LiveStatus flow) so the summary rides the reliable clip pattern instead of an engine-dependent zero-area image. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Correctness]** qham full and tapered modes read different bond lengths - live in the shipped lesson at R=0.74 — Full mode snaps to the nearest fixture point (nearestPoint) while tapered mode linearly interpolates at the exact slider/fence R (h2OneQubit). The shipped 05-quantum-chemistry lesson fence passes R=0.74, which is off the fixture's 0.05 A grid: full mode shows the 15 JW coefficients sampled at R=0.75, tapered mode shows c0/cz/cx interpolated at 0.74, and the two differ in the SECOND decimal (c0 -0.3387 vs -0.3262, cz +0.7775 vs +0.7902) - fully visible at the widget's 4-decimal display. The toggle that teaches 'tapering folds THE 4-qubit operator down to 1 qubit' therefore compares two different Hamiltonians. Compounding it, the always-visible caption 'sampled at R = 0.75 A' is false in tapered mode (those bars are interpolated at 0.74, not sampled), and LiveStatus/listLabel announce 'at R = 0.74' in full mode while the bars carry 0.75 data. _Fix:_ Source both modes from the same fixture row: the points already carry c0/cz/cx, so derive taperedCoeffs from point (point.c0/cz/cx or h2OneQubit(point.R, ...)) instead of the raw R, and announce point.R in LiveStatus/listLabel. Then the tapered toggle is an exact folding of the displayed full operator, and 'sampled at R = 0.75 A' is truthful in both modes. Add a test rendering R=0.74 tapered and asserting the announced c0 equals the R=0.75 fixture value. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Correctness]** qpes labels the right-edge FCI reading as the 'dissociation asymptote' and derives well depth from it — geom.asymptote and geom.wellDepth are computed from the FCI value at the fixture's last point (R=2.7 A, -0.934584 Ha), but H2 is not yet dissociated there: the curve still rises 0.28 mHa over the final 0.05 A, and the true STO-3G dissociation limit 2*E(H,STO-3G) = -0.93316 Ha lies 1.4 mHa ABOVE the drawn dashed line. The readout panel prints 'asymptote -0.9346 Ha' and 'well depth 0.2025 Ha' where the actual STO-3G FCI values are -0.9332 and 0.2040 Ha (~0.7% off), and the drawn line makes the curve appear to have reached its asymptote at the plot's right edge. For a widget that carries an explicit 'Honesty note' about basis-set limitations, presenting a finite-R reading as the asymptote is the one remaining imprecision. _Fix:_ Relabel the two readouts to what they are - e.g. 'FCI at R = 2.7 A' (or 'asymptote (approx)') and 'well depth (to 2.7 A)' - or extend the honesty note with one clause: 'the curve at 2.7 A sits ~1.4 mHa below the true STO-3G limit 2E(H)'. Pure label/copy change; no new data or functionality. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Resilience & tests]** The JW explorer's rendered (X ± iY)/2 sign has no view-level regression test — The one convention-critical rendering in JwExplorer left unpinned is the sign in the displayed formula: sign = image.ySign &lt; 0 ? '−' : '+' maps a_p-dagger to (X − iY)/2 and a_p to (X + iY)/2. jw.test.ts pins ySign at the model layer, and jw-explorer.test.tsx pins the phantom-Z regression and the annihilation announcement, but no test asserts the rendered minus/plus - so a view-side regression (flipping the ternary or consuming ySign backwards) would silently render a_p-dagger = (X + iY)/2, which IS the annihilator: exactly the class of sign-convention miseducation this widget exists to prevent, invisible to every current test. _Fix:_ Add two assertions to jw-explorer.test.tsx: render with dagger true and assert the formula contains 'X −' (unicode minus, as rendered) next to iY; click the annihilation toggle and assert 'X +'. One test, pins the last untested link in the sign chain. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** The H2 fixture's bond-length domain is re-derived six times across the cluster, including a useMemo over module constants — All three fixture-driven explorers independently recompute the fixture's R domain from the module-constant H2 object: each parseSource reads points[0].R / points[points.length-1].R for range validation, and hamiltonian-explorer recomputes minR/maxR a second time in the component body plus derives the slider pitch (stepR) inside a useMemo with an empty dependency array — a hook spent memoizing a value that never changes and could be a module constant. The duplication is code noise and each new fixture-driven widget re-derives the same three numbers again. _Fix:_ Export the domain once next to the fixture (e.g. R_MIN, R_MAX, and the sampling pitch in h2-data.ts, computed at module load); the three parseSource range checks and hamiltonian-explorer's component-body copies read the constants, and the stepR useMemo is deleted outright. - [x] **[Cleaner code]** vqe-explorer's error card carries an unreachable "no model" message branch — The model memo returns null exactly and only when parsing failed (its first line is `if (!parsed.ok) return null`), so in the guard `if (!parsed.ok || !model)` the combination parsed.ok && !model is impossible — the user-facing string "no model" in `message={parsed.ok ? "no model" : parsed.error}` is dead code that exists solely to satisfy TypeScript narrowing, and would surface as the confusing card "qvqe error: no model" if a future edit ever made it reachable. _Fix:_ Split the guard into two: `if (!parsed.ok) return <ErrorCard message={parsed.error} />;` followed by `if (!model) return null; // unreachable — narrows the type` — removing the fake user-facing string while keeping the compiler satisfied. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:105-106`, `web/src/components/quantum/vqe-explorer.tsx:133-135`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)

WidgetCard already exposes eyebrowAs="h3" and eyebrowId for exactly this (checkpoint-explorer uses it), so the widget carries a hand-rolled copy of a capability the shared shell provides. The same stale pattern exists in metrics-explorer and vqc-trainer (outside this feature's scope, worth a cross-reference). _Fix:_ Pass eyebrowAs="h3" to the WidgetCard and delete the sr-only h3 plus the unused headingId useId — one heading, sourced from the shared shell, no dead id. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Accessibility]** Grover slider double-announces the same success readout via aria-valuetext AND the live region — On every iterations-slider tick, GroverVisualizer pushes the identical fact through two SR channels: the slider's aria-valuetext says "N iterations, success X%" while the polite LiveStatus simultaneously re-announces "Success probability X% at N iterations." A screen-reader user dragging the primary control of this shipped widget hears each value twice. This is the exact anti-pattern the same codebase engineered against in hamiltonian-explorer, whose inline comment keeps valuetext and LiveStatus complementary because repeating content "would double-announce the same label + coefficient" — and qaoa-explorer follows that complementary split (valuetext = angle only, LiveStatus = expected cut). Grover is the one F8 outlier. Not a re-report of the closed WS-6b item: the announcement itself stays; only the duplication between the two channels is the defect. _Fix:_ Make the two channels complementary per the hamiltonian-explorer convention: keep the rich LiveStatus (it also serves the qubits/marked selects, which have no valuetext) and trim the slider's ariaValueText to the input it drives (e.g. "3 iterations"), or vice versa — one channel owns the computed success%. Update the pinned assertion in grover-visualizer.test.tsx accordingly. Impact: Low. Effort: Low.
