- [x] **[UI polish & performance (#169 drift)]** bar-shimmer is declared one-shot in three comments but animates infinite — every data bar glints forever — PR #169 (yesterday) added a shimmer sweep to the shared Bar fill, applied unconditionally to every probability/histogram/tally bar in the F6 family (ProbBars in CircuitLab/BlochBuilder/Scrubber, ShotsSampler's histogram, CorrelationDemo's tallies). The keyframes comment, the utility comment, and the reduced-motion override comment all say "One-shot shimmer sweep across a data bar after it settles" — but the declaration is `animation: bar-shimmer 2.8s ease-in-out infinite`, so every bar re-sweeps a 40%-white gradient every 2.8 seconds forever. On a lesson page with several open widgets that is a perpetual glint across dozens of data readouts plus never-idle compositor work; there is also no "after it settles" logic — it fires 1.1s after mount regardless. Either the implementation or the thrice-stated intent is wrong; they cannot both stand. _Fix:_ Make the behavior match the stated intent: `animation: bar-shimmer 2.8s ease-in-out 1.1s 1 both` (one sweep, then hold off-bar) — or, if the design verdict is that the ambient loop IS the smoke-and-glass intent, rewrite the three "one-shot" comments so the next reader does not fix it the other way. One-shot is the better default: a repeating specular pass over data-encoding bars reads as activity where none exists and keeps the compositor busy on otherwise idle lesson pages. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Consistency]** Eight widgets hand-roll the ErrorCard body that widget-ui already exports, and the copies have drifted on text color and margin — widget-ui.tsx exports `ErrorCard`, adopted by ~20 widgets. But seven error branches inline the exact same div/p structure instead, and predict-widget goes further and defines its own local `function ErrorCard` that shadows the shared name inside that module. The inlined copies are not byte-identical to the shared one: they use `text-caption` where the shared component uses `text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400`, and `my-8` where the shared default is `my-6`. So a parse error in ```qsim (shared ErrorCard, my-6, gray-500) and a parse error in ```qchallenge (inlined, my-8, --mut) render at different vertical rhythm and different muted tints for what is the same failure state. _Fix:_ Delete predict-widget's local ErrorCard and the seven inlined blocks; call the shared `ErrorCard label="<fence token>"` at each site, passing `className="my-8"` where the extra margin is wanted (scrolly-section.tsx:40 already demonstrates that override). That also removes the name shadowing that currently makes `ErrorCard` mean two different components inside components/quantum. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Accessibility]** circuit-lab and bloch-builder wrap the probability bars, the full Dirac string, and two nested live regions in one un-gated polite region driven by a 60-120 step slider — Both widgets put `ProbBars` + `StateReadout` inside a single `role="status" aria-live="polite"` div. Every slider tick rewrites all of that text, so a screen-reader user dragging theta or phi gets the whole basis-state probability list plus the complete Dirac string re-announced per step (theta = pi/60 -&gt; 60 steps; phi = 2pi/60 -&gt; 120 steps). Worse, StateReadout renders two CopyButtons, each of which contains its own `role="status" aria-live="polite"` span — so these are nested live regions, an ARIA pattern with implementation-defined behavior: the outer region's per-tick mutation re-announces the inner region's subtree, and a "Copied" announcement also dirties the outer region. The repo has already codified the opposite rule in two places, so these two widgets are the outliers, not the norm. _Fix:_ Apply the noise-visualizer pattern already proven in this codebase: drop `role="status" aria-live="polite"` from the wrapper divs in circuit-lab.tsx:47 and bloch-builder-widget.tsx:49 (which also un-nests the CopyButton regions), and instead render one `<LiveStatus>` sibling carrying a single concise line derived from the same state — e.g. `P(0) 50 percent, P(1) 50 percent` for the builder, or the dominant-amplitude summary for the lab. That keeps the announcement the WS-5a/2026-06-17 sweep intended while collapsing a per-tick paragraph into one short sentence and removing the nested-region ambiguity. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/circuit-lab.tsx:47-50`, `web/src/components/quantum/bloch-builder-widget.tsx:49-52`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:164,166`, `web/src/components/copy-button.tsx:103-105`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** parseProgram silently drops extra tokens on every instruction, so a mistyped circuit grades "wrong" with no diagnostic — `parseProgram` reads only `parts[0..2]` and never checks how many tokens a line actually has. `H 0 1` (a natural typo for "H on qubits 0 and 1"), `CNOT 0 1 2` (a learner reaching for a Toffoli), and `RY 0 theta 5` all parse successfully as one-and-two-token instructions with the trailing tokens discarded. This is the one malformed-input class the DSL does NOT reject, and it is the worst one to accept silently: the parser is deliberately fail-loud on every other bad token — parse-utils.ts:60-63 explains that it rejects `parseInt("0abc")` precisely because it "would otherwise build a wrong (or crash-on-silent) circuit" — and extra tokens produce exactly that same wrong-but-silent circuit. Because `parseProgram` feeds the graders, the learner's dropped token becomes an unexplainable rejection: challenge-grade.ts returns a precise `status:"error"` only when `learner.error` is set, so a token-dropped circuit falls through to the generic `status:"wrong"` / "Not quite — the resulting state doesn't match the target yet." The learner is told their quantum reasoning is wrong when in fact their syntax was silently truncated. In authored lesson content the same gap ships a teaching example whose rendered state differs from the source the reader is looking at. _Fix:_ Add the missing arity check to the three gate branches in parseProgram: reject any instruction carrying more tokens than it consumes, with the same fail-loud message style the parser already uses (e.g. ``throw new Error(`${gate}: unexpected extra token "${parts[expected]}"`)`` for SINGLE/ROT/CNOT, and the qubits directive). This turns a silent wrong-circuit into the precise `status:"error"` message challenge-grade.ts already knows how to surface. Add the mirror cases to qsim-dsl.test.ts alongside the existing garbage-token cases. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/qsim-dsl.ts:53`, `web/src/components/quantum/parse-utils.ts:60-63`, `web/src/lib/challenge-grade.ts:19-22`, `web/src/lib/challenge-grade.ts:69-74`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Performance]** The "one-shot" bar shimmer is declared `infinite`, so every probability bar animates forever — including the zero-width ones — `Bar` unconditionally applies the `bar-shimmer` class to every fill span, and the CSS declares `animation: bar-shimmer 2.8s ease-in-out infinite`. Three separate comments in globals.css describe this as a ONE-SHOT sweep ("One-shot shimmer sweep across a data bar after it settles", "One-shot shimmer sweep overlay", "One-shot shimmer never runs under reduced-motion"), so the `infinite` keyword contradicts the documented design intent in all three places. The cost scales with basis states, not with widgets: `ProbBars` renders one `Bar` per amplitude, so a 4-qubit circuit yields 16 perpetually-animating pseudo-elements — 15 of which have a zero-width fill and are therefore invisible while still ticking. Seventeen `Bar`/`ProbBars` call sites across sixteen files means a lesson carrying several circuit widgets holds dozens of never-terminating compositor animations, keeping the compositor awake for the whole session on content that is visually static. Reduced-motion users are correctly exempt (globals.css:685 and the `animation-iteration-count: 1 !important` catch-all), so this lands entirely on the default-motion majority. _Fix:_ Change `infinite` to a single iteration at globals.css:563 so the declaration matches the "one-shot ... after it settles" contract its three comments already state — the sweep still plays once after the 1.1s delay and then stops, leaving no standing compositor work. If the perpetual loop turns out to be deliberate, the three comments should be corrected instead so the next reader is not misled; either way the code and its documented intent must agree. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:111`, `web/src/app/globals.css:563`, `web/src/app/globals.css:556`, `web/src/app/globals.css:117`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** Both QAOA landscape markers — "you are here" and "the optimum" — fall below the 3:1 non-text floor, and the max marker is structurally guaranteed to land on its own worst-case cell — The landscape's entire teaching payload is two marks on the heatmap: the amber square on the grid maximum and the dot showing the current (gamma, beta). Neither is legible against the cell it sits on. The grid-max marker is the severe one, because its bad contrast is not incidental — it is guaranteed by construction. `heatColor((v - gridMax.lo) / span)` renders a cell at 100% `var(--accent)` exactly when `v === gridMax.value`, and the amber rect is drawn at precisely that cell. So the marker is always painted on the single most accent-saturated tile in the grid. Measured: amber-400 on dark `--accent` = 1.11:1; amber-500 on light `--accent` = 1.42:1. It fails in BOTH themes, and it fails hardest at the one place a learner looks. The current-position dot fails only in dark, but that is where it matters: `dark:fill-white` on the accent peak = 1.55:1. Its `stroke="currentColor"` is meant to separate it, but the circle sets no color class and no ancestor does either, so currentColor resolves to the body's `--ink` — 1.12:1 against the dot's own white fill and 1.38:1 against the cell. The separating ring is a no-op. (Light theme is fine at 5.83:1, which is why this reads as clean in a light-mode screenshot.) The repo already has the right idiom eight lines up: `GraphSvg`'s vertex circles carry `stroke-white dark:stroke-gray-900`, a theme-inverted halo. The landscape markers never got it. _Fix:_ Give both marks the theme-inverted halo the graph vertices already use. For the current dot, replace `stroke="currentColor"` with the explicit `stroke-white dark:stroke-gray-900` pairing so the ring inverts against the fill in each theme. For the grid-max square, draw it as two stacked rects — a slightly wider `stroke-white dark:stroke-gray-900` under-stroke, then the amber hairline on top — so it reads against a fully accent-saturated cell in both themes. Then extend token-contrast.test.ts's 3:1 non-text tier with the two marker-vs-`--accent` pairings, since the failure is a token pairing and only a computed guard will hold it. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:238`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:104-108`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:242-243`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:289-296`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Accessibility]** The QAOA graph's vertex numerals are white-on-accent at 3.04:1 — the exact pairing contrast-guard.test.ts exists to block, in a form its grep structurally cannot see — `GraphSvg` fills each vertex circle with `fill-accent` and paints the vertex number `fill-white` at `fontSize={9}` semibold. In the light theme that is white on `--accent` = oklch(0.66 0.1 118), which computes to 3.04:1. AA for text at that size is 4.5:1. This is precisely the failure `contrast-guard.test.ts` was written to prevent — its docstring says white text on a solid accent fill is the sub-AA pairing, and PR #72 introduced `.surface-accent` to retire it site-wide. But the guard matches `/\b(?:bg|from|via|to)-accent\b/` against `/\btext-white\b/` **on the same line**. Here the pairing is expressed as SVG paint utilities (`fill-accent`, `fill-white`) on two different lines, nine lines apart. The guard cannot fire on it in principle, not just by accident — so the sweep that cleaned up every `bg-`/`text-` instance left this one standing and nothing will ever flag it. It matters more than a decorative contrast miss because the numerals carry `aria-hidden="true"`: there is no text alternative, so visual legibility is the only channel. And the widget's own caption — "bit order: vertex 0 on the left (matches the graph)" — instructs the learner to map distribution bits onto exactly these numerals. If they cannot read them, the distribution panel loses its referent. The dark theme is fine (gray-900 on `--accent-light` = 13.40:1); this is a light-theme-only failure, which is why a dark-mode review pass would miss it. _Fix:_ Route the vertex numeral through a legible token instead of raw white — e.g. fill it with the same near-black/near-white inversion `.surface-accent` uses (`var(--btn-ink)`-style), or deepen the node fill to `fill-accent-dark` in light so white clears 4.5:1. Then widen contrast-guard.test.ts to also treat `fill-accent` as a solid accent fill and `fill-white` as white text, and match across a small line window (or per-file) rather than per-line, so the SVG expression of this pairing cannot reappear. Apply the same fix to topology-explorer.tsx:234/247, which carries the identical pairing. Impact: Medium. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:158`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:161-171`, `web/src/app/globals.css:20`, `web/__tests__/contrast-guard.test.ts:29-30`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Consistency]** ProbBars — the highest-traffic percent readout in the family — still hand-rolls its percent format instead of using formatPercent — PR #79 created formatPercent in format.ts to single-source percent readouts and swept 32 call sites, but never touched widget-ui.tsx (git show 3f1c23d confirms), so the shared ProbBars used by CircuitLab, WavefunctionScrubber, and BlochBuilder on every slider tick still inlines `${(p * 100).toFixed(1)}%`. Behavior is identical today (probabilities are non-negative so formatFixed's -0 snap never fires), making this pure convention drift inside the shared module itself — the one place the convention should be authoritative. _Fix:_ Import formatPercent in widget-ui.tsx and render valueText={formatPercent(p * 100)} in ProbBars. Zero visual change; closes the last percent readout the #79 consolidation missed. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Consistency]** dj-demo's Oracle select hand-rolls field + focus styling, bypassing fieldClass and the site-wide .focus-ring/--focus token — The qdj oracle select is the last explorable form control carrying the alpha focus ring the improve-ui sweep removed: it uses focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent/40 (an alpha ring, and plain focus: rather than focus-visible:) plus rounded-md, instead of the shared .focus-ring utility that PR #172 just re-pointed at the per-theme solid --focus token, and instead of the shared fieldClass (or the token-based select recipe its grover sibling uses). globals.css documents exactly why the alpha ring was retired: it composites below the WCAG 1.4.11 3:1 floor on light surfaces. The select also carries a redundant aria-label="Oracle" that overrides the visible &lt;label htmlFor&gt; of identical text. _Fix:_ Restyle the select to the sibling recipe: rounded-control + focus-ring (or fieldClass + per-control sizing), dropping focus:outline-none/focus:ring-2/focus:ring-accent/40 and rounded-md, and delete the redundant aria-label. This re-unifies dj-demo with the per-theme --focus token so future theme retunes reach it. No test pins these classes (dj-demo.test.tsx only pins verdict text and the .text-caption footnote). Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[Correctness]** QFT input/output panels are each normalized to their own peak, so cross-panel bar lengths silently lie — the shipped period:4 config is the one case that masks it — MagnitudeBars computes `peak = Math.max(...values)` per panel and renders `fraction = v / peak`, so the left (input) and right (output) panels use different hidden scales whenever their peak magnitudes differ. For `periodicState`, input teeth have magnitude 1/√(N/r) while output spikes have 1/√r — equal only when r = √N. The lesson fence uses qubits:4, period:4 (exactly r = √16), the unique config where the display is honest. Any other valid config distorts: period:2 on 4 qubits renders 0.35-magnitude input teeth and 0.71-magnitude output spikes at identical full track width; the documented `{"basis": j}` mode renders sixteen 0.25-magnitude output bars all at 100% width beside a single 1.0-magnitude input bar of the same length. In a widget whose whole lesson is 'watch a periodic comb become a frequency comb', bar length is the teaching signal, and it does not commute across the two side-by-side panels. _Fix:_ Use one scale for both panels: either render absolute magnitudes (`fraction={v}` — all magnitudes are ≤ 1 by normalization, matching the sibling widgets' absolute ProbBars convention) or divide both panels by the shared max across inMag and outMag. While touching the panel, the header 'Input |x⟩' (qft-visualizer.tsx:164) mislabels the default period-comb superposition as a basis state — 'Input state' fixes it. Pure refinement of existing rendering; no new features. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/qft-visualizer.tsx:100`, `web/src/components/quantum/qft-visualizer.tsx:114`, `web/src/components/quantum/qft-visualizer.tsx:161-173`, `web/src/components/quantum/qft.ts:36`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)

This is the one hand-rolled copy of an F12 primitive the adoption sweeps missed (every range input inside components/quantum/ goes through LabeledSlider). _Fix:_ Either swap the two rows to LabeledSlider (it already supports labelAbove-style layouts and the rows match its shape), or minimally append `focus-ring` to both inputs so pricing sliders join the per-theme --focus treatment. Also inherits the fixes from the two .slider findings above once those land. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. - [x] **[UX]** ErrorCard renders a failed widget as a muted caption — the design system's warm failure tier goes unused where failure is the whole message — When a widget fence fails to parse, ErrorCard replaces the entire explorable with `label error: message` in text-gray-500 inside an ordinary glass card — visually identical to footnote text, with no cue that something is broken rather than merely annotated. The system already has an established failure treatment (--color-warm: expectation/cost-estimate verdict chips use bg-warm/10 text-warm-dark, CopyButton's failed state uses text-warm-dark), so a quiet cool-gray line is both off-pattern and easy to read past for a learner who hits a broken lesson. Minor edge: when message is undefined the template renders a dangling 'label error: '. _Fix:_ Restyle ErrorCard's text to the warm failure tier (text-warm-dark dark:text-warm-light, optionally a warm hairline via border-warm/20) so a broken widget is visually distinct from a caption, and drop the dangling colon when message is absent. ~15 widget error paths inherit it from the one component; no behavior change, ErrorCard's test only asserts the text content and cardShell classes. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:257-263`, `web/src/components/copy-button.tsx:96-99`, `web/src/components/quantum/expectation-widget.tsx:141-145`, `web/src/app/globals.css:31-33`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UX]** DJ demo never visually distinguishes the decisive |00...0&gt; row, unlike every sibling that highlights its key state — The whole Deutsch-Jozsa teaching point is one bar: the all-zeros probability (the footnote says "all-zeros with certainty means the function never varies", and the sr-only LiveStatus announces exactly that number). But the widget renders plain ProbBars where all 8 rows (n=3) get the identical accent fill — nothing marks row |000&gt; as the one to watch. Both siblings in this feature already established the highlight pattern: grover renders the marked state with bar-fill + bold accent label while unmarked rows are neutral gray, and qft paints spike rows bg-warm. A learner toggling constant vs balanced oracles has to know in advance which of 8 visually identical rows carries the verdict. _Fix:_ Render the DJ bars the way grover already does: map result.probs through the shared Bar primitive, giving idx===0 the accent fill + semibold accent label and the other rows the neutral fill. Pure restyling of existing rows with existing primitives — no new controls or data. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/dj-demo.tsx:110`, `web/src/components/quantum/dj-demo.tsx:112-115`, `web/src/components/quantum/grover-visualizer.tsx:107-124`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[UI]** Token drift inside F8: QAOA readouts on raw grays and amber-500 where siblings use --mut/warm tokens, and grover/qft disagree on the neutral bar fill — Within the four algorithm widgets the same semantic roles pull from different color sources, so the set reads as three generations of styling. (a) qaoa-explorer's expected-cut readout and slider labels use raw palette grays (text-gray-800 dark:text-gray-200, text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-300) while grover expresses the identical roles with the Instrument tokens (text-(--mut), text-caption). (b) qaoa's grid-max marker is raw text-amber-500 dark:text-amber-400 while the design system already has the warm token family (globals.css --color-warm*, hue 75) used for exactly this 'hot/optimum highlight' role by qft's spike bars and dj's Balanced chip. (c) The de-emphasized neutral bar fill differs between siblings: grover's unmarked rows are bg-gray-300 dark:bg-gray-600 but qft's non-highlight bars are bg-gray-400 dark:bg-gray-500 — same role, two grays, visible when the widgets appear in the same lesson. _Fix:_ One alignment sweep across the four files: qaoa readout/labels -&gt; text-(--ink)/text-(--mut)/text-caption matching grover; qaoa's amber marker -&gt; the warm token (text-warm-dark dark:text-warm, or stroke via --color-warm); pick one neutral bar fill (grover's or qft's) and use it in both. Zero behavior change, and it brings F8 fully onto the tokens the new token-contrast test suite guards. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:306-309`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:324`, `web/src/components/quantum/qaoa-explorer.tsx:242-243`, `web/src/components/quantum/grover-visualizer.tsx:115`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** qsim DSL silently ignores extra tokens, so 'H 0 1' builds a circuit that differs from the learner's intent — parseProgram reads only the tokens each branch expects and never checks parts.length, so trailing tokens are silently dropped. A learner in the editable CircuitLab who types 'H 0 1' (a natural spelling of 'H on qubits 0 and 1') gets H applied to qubit 0 only, sees |00&gt;+|10&gt; in the readout, and can walk away believing that is what H-on-both-qubits produces. Same for 'CNOT 0 1 2' and 'RY 0 theta 42'. This defeats the parser's own hardening theme: parse-utils deliberately rejects parseInt/parseFloat silent truncation ('H 2x', 'RY 0 1.5xyz') precisely to prevent wrong-but-silent circuits, yet a whole extra token slips through the same door. _Fix:_ In each parse branch, after consuming the expected tokens, throw when parts.length exceeds the branch's arity (e.g. `if (parts.length > 2) throw new Error(`${gate} takes exactly one qubit — one instruction per line`)`), reusing the existing try/catch -&gt; Program.error path so the ErrorCard renders the message. Add qsim-dsl.test.ts cases for 'H 0 1', 'CNOT 0 1 2', 'RY 0 theta 42', and 'qubits 2 3'. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/qsim-dsl.ts:89-94`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/qsim-dsl.ts:65-73`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/parse-utils.ts:60-64`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Resilience & tests]** LabeledSlider is the only widget-ui primitive with zero direct unit coverage — widget-ui.test.tsx pins every other export (GateChip, StateReadout, LiveStatus, Bar, ProbBars, EyebrowLabel, Chip, WidgetCard, ErrorCard, fieldClass, primaryActionClass) but never renders LabeledSlider — and no other test file references it (grep across web/__tests__ finds zero hits). Its contract points are therefore unpinned: the useId htmlFor&lt;-&gt;input wiring (this cannot be caught by any widget-level getByLabelText test because the input also carries aria-label, which wins the accessible-name computation — a broken htmlFor would silently kill click-label-to-focus), the parse hook (default parseFloat vs callers passing parseInt), aria-valuetext pass-through, and the labelAbove/leading layout branches. The component is consumed by ~19 files (every quantum explorer plus the playground panels), so a regression propagates platform-wide with no failing test. _Fix:_ Add a LabeledSlider describe block to widget-ui.test.tsx mirroring the sibling suites: (1) the rendered &lt;label&gt; htmlFor matches the input id (query the label element directly, not via accessible name, since aria-label masks it); (2) fireEvent.change delivers parse(raw) — assert parseFloat default and a parseInt override; (3) aria-valuetext lands on the input; (4) labelAbove renders label then the flex row, and leading injects before the input; (5) label={undefined} renders no &lt;label&gt; and relies on aria-label. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/__tests__/components/quantum/widget-ui.test.tsx:6-18`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:298-382`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Correctness]** gateLabel renders a '-0.00' chip for tiny negative literal angles, bypassing the formatFixed -0 snap — gateLabel formats a literal rotation angle with raw theta.toFixed(2). For a small negative literal ('RZ 0 -0.001', legal per parseAngle which deliberately accepts negative rotations), the chip reads 'RZ(-0.00) q0' — the signed-zero display wart the #79 formatting campaign eradicated from 32 readouts by introducing formatFixed, which snaps sub-half-ULP values to exact 0 before toFixed. Verified in node: Number('-0.001').toFixed(2) === '-0.00'. A '-0.00' angle label is exactly the class of sign-display noise the shared format module exists to prevent, and gate chips render in every circuit-family explorable plus the playground. _Fix:_ In gateLabel, replace g.theta.toFixed(2) with formatFixed(g.theta, 2) from ./format (label stays '1.57'-style for normal angles, '0.00' instead of '-0.00' at the edge), and add a widget-ui.test.tsx case pinning gateLabel({gate:'RZ', target:0, theta:-0.001}) === 'RZ(0.00) q0'. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:24`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/format.ts:1-4`, `/Users/cperez/dev/altivum-dev/quantum/web/src/components/quantum/parse-utils.ts:77-84`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Cleaner code]** math.ts applyGate1 duplicates applyGate1InPlace's butterfly loop verbatim — The pure and in-place single-qubit gate applications carry two identical copies of the stride/butterfly loop; the only difference is that applyGate1 first deep-copies the state. Duplicated kernel math is a drift hazard - a fix to one loop (index math, gate-element order) can silently miss the other. _Fix:_ Implement applyGate1 as `return applyGate1InPlace(state.map((c) => [c[0], c[1]] as Complex), gate, qubit, n)` - one loop to maintain, identical results (math.test.ts and the qcsim fixture parity suite pin the behavior). Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/math.ts:76-89`, `web/src/components/quantum/math.ts:97-109`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Cleaner code]** CheckIcon is defined six times, byte-identically, inside the quantum widget family — The same 5-line SVG check glyph is redeclared as a private component in six widget modules. Because each copy is module-private, none of them is code-shared across the dynamic-import chunk boundary — the identical markup ships in six separate widget chunks — and a change to the icon (stroke width, size, the `aria-hidden` that keeps it out of the accessible name) has to be made in six places or the widgets diverge. _Fix:_ Export a single `CheckIcon` from widget-ui.tsx alongside GateChip/Chip and import it in the six widgets. Keep the size a prop default (`h-3.5 w-3.5`) so copy-button and section-progress can later adopt it too without changing their glyph size. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/challenge.tsx:21-27`, `web/src/components/quantum/debug-circuit-widget.tsx:23-29`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:29`, `web/src/components/copy-button.tsx:22`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Cleaner code]** cardShell is exported but has zero importers, while eleven widgets hand-write the shell string plus a shadow that .glass already applies — widget-ui exports `cardShell` as the canonical shell recipe and deliberately omits the shadow, because the `.glass` utility already sets `box-shadow: var(--shadow-resting)`. No file outside widget-ui imports it. Instead eleven widget modules write the shell string by hand and every one of them appends a redundant `shadow-(--shadow-resting)` that re-declares the shadow `.glass` has already applied. The result is a shared token nobody uses, plus fifteen-odd copies of a class string with a no-op utility welded on — and any future change to the shell recipe has to be made in twelve places. _Fix:_ Import `cardShell` at the eleven sites and compose (`${cardShell} not-prose my-8 overflow-hidden`), dropping the redundant shadow utility. Better still, most of these are full widget shells that could pass through `WidgetCard` — twenty-seven widgets already do — which would also fix the eyebrow drift in finding 1 as a side effect. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:175-177`, `web/src/app/globals.css:345-349`, `web/src/components/quantum/predict-widget.tsx:27-28`, `web/src/components/quantum/challenge.tsx:150`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Cleaner code]** Two spellings of the same muted-text token are used roughly 50/50 inside the widget directory, including inside widget-ui itself — `.text-caption` is defined as nothing more than `color: var(--mut)`, which is exactly what the `text-(--mut)` arbitrary-value utility produces. Both spellings are in heavy use in components/quantum — 124 occurrences of one, 104 of the other — for identical rendered output. widget-ui.tsx mixes them within a single file, which means the shared module gives no signal about which one is canonical, and every new widget picks whichever it copied from. Nothing distinguishes the two beyond authorship history. _Fix:_ Declare one canonical spelling in the widget-ui header comment (`text-caption` reads better and is the semantic name) and sweep the directory to it, so a widget author copying any neighbouring file lands on the same token. Purely mechanical with zero rendered-output change, which also makes it safe to verify by snapshot. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/app/globals.css:546-548`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:68-69`, `web/src/components/quantum/widget-ui.tsx:160`, `web/src/components/quantum/grover-visualizer.tsx:119-131`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)
- [x] **[Performance]** use-display-caps re-subscribes the reduced-motion listener on every render of every consumer, defeating the file's own memoization — Both hooks pass inline closures to `useSyncExternalStore`. React stores the subscription as an effect with deps `[subscribe]` (verified in the installed react-dom 19.2.4 source: `updateEffectImpl(2048, Passive, create, [subscribe])`), so a fresh closure identity on every render tears down and re-creates the subscription every single commit. For `usePrefersReducedMotion` that means a real `removeEventListener` + `addEventListener` pair on the MediaQueryList on every render of every consumer; its `getSnapshot` is also inline, which additionally trips the `hook.getSnapshot !== getSnapshot` branch and pushes a passive effect each render. This is precisely the per-render waste the module header was written to eliminate — the file already memoizes the MediaQueryList and the WebGL probe at module scope "because React calls getSnapshot on every render of every consumer... these hooks feed hot-re-rendering widgets (Bloch builder, scrubber, scrolly)" — but the closures themselves were left inline, so the churn moved rather than disappeared. Those hot consumers are exactly the slider-driven ones that re-render on every drag frame. The codebase already has the correct idiom in the same directory: use-persistent-solved.ts passes a module-level `subscribe` import. _Fix:_ Hoist the closures to module scope alongside the already-memoized probes: a `subscribeReducedMotion(onChange)` function, a `getReducedMotion()` snapshot, a shared no-op `subscribeNever`, and a shared `serverFalse` — then pass those stable references into both useSyncExternalStore calls. Extend use-display-caps.test.tsx to assert the mock's `addEventListener` is called at most once across the six rerenders it already performs, which pins the fix in the same shape as the existing memoization test. Impact: Low. Effort: Low. (`web/src/components/quantum/use-display-caps.ts:32-36`, `web/src/components/quantum/use-display-caps.ts:37`, `web/src/components/quantum/use-display-caps.ts:63`, `web/src/components/quantum/use-display-caps.ts:8-13`) — added 2026-07-19 — DONE 2026-07-19 (PR pending, Category B)

### Cross-cutting (Category B): missing live regions
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-17 — **Interactive explorable readouts now announce via live regions.** Added `role="status" aria-live="polite"` to the result block of bloch-builder, circuit-lab, wavefunction-scrubber, noise (fidelity), vqc-trainer (step/loss/acc), cost-calculator (total), and topology (SWAP count); PES extends the slider `aria-valuetext` to read FCI/HF/gap. 8 widgets, 2 regression tests. Impact: High. Effort: Medium.

_New cross-feature findings (2026-06-25 deep audit — the four sweeps that a per-feature reviewer structurally cannot see):_
- [x] **[Accessibility]** DONE 2026-06-25 (WS-6b, PR #62 merged) — **Control-driven recomputation is silent to SR in the select/Run-driven explorables that WS-3 skipped.** WS-3 covered slider- and Measure-button widgets, but the `<select>`- and Run-driven ones were skipped entirely: **shots-sampler, device-table, dj-demo, grover-visualizer, qft-visualizer, encoding-explorer, vqe-explorer, jw-explorer, hamiltonian-explorer, checkpoint-explorer, param-compile-explorer** contain no `aria-live`/`role=status`/`sr-only` region, so changing a select / pressing Run / clicking Optimize announces only the control, never the recomputed result. Where a slider *does* set `aria-valuetext`, it carries the raw input, not the computed outcome. Fix: extract one tiny shared `LiveStatus` helper into `widget-ui.tsx` and add an sr-only `role=status aria-live=polite` summary to each, and enrich the grover/encoding slider `aria-valuetext` with the computed result (as `vqe`/`pes` already do). Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium. (the 11 widgets above; pattern at `correlation-demo.tsx:134`, `metrics-explorer.tsx:359`) — added 2026-06-25

PRs #34-46 centralized `ErrorCard`/`ProbBars` but never the card/header itself, and the header has already drifted (`flex flex-wrap` vs `justify-between`). Add the three primitives to `widget-ui.tsx`. Impact: Medium. Effort: Medium.
